Database Artificial Intelligence Legislation Database

Updated April 01, 2026
A Database lets you search statutes and more across the states, territories and D.C. for the latest information on a wide range of policy topics.
Related Topic: Technology

NCSL’s Artificial Intelligence Database tracks legislation regarding AI policy across state legislatures including topics relating to AI funding, government use, private sector use, AI in healthcare, responsible use and discrimination, studies, and other related topics. It reviews enacted and pending legislation beginning in 2025 and is updated monthly as legislation is identified by NCSL staff. 

  • Years Tracked: 2025-present
  • Update Frequency: Monthly
  • Legislation Included: All introduced

Topic Descriptions

User Guidance

For faster performance, please use the fields below to filter your results. If you select nothing, the default search will pick up all topics and states in the most recent session year available. The full text of bills is available by clicking on the bill number.

Topics

Status
Bill Number
Year
Author

AZ H 2311
2026
Artificial Intelligence Service
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/23/2026
Author: Rivero (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that each operator, defined as a person that makes available a conversational artificial intelligence service, shall disclose to a minor account holder that the minor is interacting with such a service, provides that each operator shall institute measures to prevent the service from, among other things, producing visual material of sexual conduct to minor account holders and from generating statements that would lead a reasonable person to believe that the person is interacting with a human.

AZ H 4009
2026
Data Centers
Status: Failed - House Third Reading
Date of Last Action: 2/23/2026
Author: Weninger (R)
Topics:
Summary: Provides that every specified number of years, the State Land Department shall develop or update a map that identifies the parcels of State lands that the State Land Commissioner deems are the highest and best locations for siting and developing computer data centers and data center facilities and a map that identifies the parcels that the commissioner deems are the highest and best locations for siting and developing thermal and nonthermal electric generating units, including small modular reactors.

California
CA S 300
2026
Companion Chatbots
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Padilla (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Require a companion chatbot operator to take the above actions when it has constructive knowledge that a user is a minor. Requires an operator to prevent its companion chatbot from producing or facilitating the exchange of any sexually explicit material or proposing sexually explicit conduct.

CA S 430
2026
Local Agencies: Automated Decision Systems
Status: Failed - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Cabaldon (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Imposes certain restrictions on the use of an automated decision system by a local agency to confer supportive services, permits, or licenses. Includes a prohibition on using an output from the system as the sole basis for an adverse eligibility or benefit determination affecting a natural person, except as specified. Requires the local agency to verify the accuracy of the system's outputs and to promote nondiscrimination in its use.
History: Click for History
02/18/2025 - INTRODUCED.

02/02/2026 - In SENATE. Returned to Secretary of Senate pursuant to Joint Rule 56.

CA S 574
2026
Generative Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Umberg (D)
Topics: Judicial Use
Summary: Obligates an attorney who uses generative artificial intelligence to practice law to ensure that confidential personal identifying, or other nonpublic information, is not entered into a public generative artificial intelligence system. Requires an attorney to ensure that reasonable steps are taken to verify the accuracy of generative artificial intelligence material and to correct any erroneous or hallucinated output in any material used by the attorney.
History: Click for History

CA S 607
2026
California Institutes for Science and Innovation
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Wiener (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Provides that existing law authorizes the Regents of the University of California to establish 4 California Institutes for Science and Innovation at separate campuses of the University of California for specified purposes. Provides that existing law authorizes the concentration of each institute to include, among other concentrations, medicine, bioengineering, or space. Authorizes the concentration of an institute to include artificial intelligence.
History: Click for History

CA S 719
2026
Department of Technology: Inventory
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 1/6/2026
Author: Cabaldon (D)
Topics: Government Use, Oversight/Governance
Summary: Provides that existing law, until January 1, 2029, requires the Department of Technology to annually submit to certain legislative committees a report regarding a specified required comprehensive inventory of all high-risk automated decision systems that have been, or are being, used, developed, or procured by a state agency. Extends that reporting requirement until January 1, 2032.

CA S 813
2026
California AI Standards and Safety Commission
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: McNerney (D)
Topics: Government Use, Oversight/Governance, Private Sector Use
Summary: Require the agency to establish the California AI Standards and Safety Commission and would provide for its membership. Requires the commission to take certain actions related to the safety of artificial intelligence, including maintaining formal liaison relationships with state agencies deploying or procuring artificial intelligence, providing artificial intelligence technical expertise and artificial intelligence risk assessment.

CA S 867
2026
Toys: Companion Chatbots
Status: Pending - Senate Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/23/2026
Author: Padilla (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits, until 1/1/2031, the manufacture, sale, exchange, possession with intent to sell or exchange, and exposition or offer for sale or exchange to a retailer a toy, as defined, that includes a companion chatbot, and would make violations of the bill subject to the same civil liability as violations of the provisions applicable to operators of companion chatbots in the above-described paragraph.

CA S 887
2026
California Environmental Quality Act: Environmental
Status: Pending - Senate Energy, Utilities and Communications Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Padilla (D)
Topics:
Summary: Provides that the Jobs and Economic Improvement Through Environmental Leadership Act of 2021 specifies the quantification and mitigation of impacts from emissions of greenhouse gases of certain environmental leadership projects. Authorizes the Governor to certify a data center project as an environmental leadership development project. Requires the quantification and mitigation of impacts for emissions of greenhouse gases of a data center project in the same manner as certain leadership projects.

CA S 903
2026
Mental Health Professionals: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Senate Business, Professions & Economic Development Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Padilla (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Prohibits a licensed professional from engaging in the use of artificial intelligence to assist in providing supplementary support in therapy or psychotherapy where the client�s therapeutic session is recorded or transcribed unless the patient or their authorized representative is informed that artificial intelligence will be used and provides consent.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.

CA S 916
2026
Civil Actions: Housing Development Projects
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/18/2026
Author: Ashby (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that existing law provides that in a civil action brought by a plaintiff to challenge a housing development project that meets or exceeds the requirements for low or moderate income housing, a defendant may seek an order requiring the plaintiff to furnish an undertaking as security for costs and damages. Provides that existing law authorizes the court to limit the amount of the undertaking or to decline that requirement under certain conditions. Applies provisions to a student housing development.
History: Click for History

CA S 928
2026
California State University: Faculty Employees
Status: Pending - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/24/2026
Author: Cervantes (D)
Topics: Government Use, Effect on Labor/Employment
Summary: Provides that existing law requires certain community college employees to be a person who meets specified minimum qualifications. Requires the instructor of record for a course of instruction to be a person who meets the rule provided by the Trustees of the California State University to serve as a faculty employee teaching credit instruction or noncredit instruction.
History: Click for History

CA S 947
2026
Employment: Automated Decision Systems
Status: Pending - Senate Labor, Public Employment and Retirement Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: McNerney (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits an employer from using an automated decision systems to perform certain functions and limits the purposes for and way in which an ADS may be used. Authorizes a worker to request, and requires an employer to provide, a copy of the most recent specified number of months of the workers own data primarily used by an ADS to make a disciplinary, termination, or deactivation decision. Applies such provisions to all cities, including charter cities.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/18/2026 - To SENATE Committees on LABOR, PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT AND RETIREMENT and PRIVACY, DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.

03/26/2026 - In SENATE. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on LABOR, PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT AND RETIREMENT.

CA S 951
2026
Employment: Technological Displacement: Notice
Status: Pending - Senate Labor, Public Employment and Retirement Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Reyes (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Establishes the California Worker Technological Displacement Act. Requires an employer to provide at least a specified number of days of advanced written notice before any technological displacement affecting a specified number or more workers or a specified percent of the workforce. Makes an employer that fails to give notice before ordering a technological displacement liable for back pay and benefits to each worker. Provides for civil penalties. Establishes the Technological Displacement Act Fund.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/18/2026 - To SENATE Committees on LABOR, PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT & RETIREMENT and PRIVACY, DIGITAL TECHNOLOGY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/26/2026 - From SENATE Committee on LABOR, PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT AND RETIREMENT with author's amendments.
03/26/2026 - In SENATE. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on LABOR, PUBLIC EMPLOYMENT AND RETIREMENT.

CA S 1000
2026
California AI Transparency Act
Status: Pending - Senate Privacy, Digital Technologies, and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Becker (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Provides that the California AI Transparency Act requires a covered provider to make available an AI detection tool at no cost to the user. Provides that existing law requires a covered provider to include a certain latent disclosure in AI generated image, video, or audio content, or content that is any combination thereof, created by the covered providers GenAI system. Requires a covered provider to include in the latent disclosure whether the content is generated or modified by artificial intelligence.
History: Click for History

CA S 1011
2026
Energy: Utility Infrastructure AI Safety, Oversight,
Status: Pending - Senate Energy, Utilities and Communications Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: McNerney (D)
Topics: Energy
Summary: Requires the Public Utilities Commission, for a privately owned utility, and the Energy Commission, for a publicly owned utility, to oversee the implementation of a specified program to regulate automated decision systems in connection with certain utility functions.
History: Click for History
02/10/2026 - INTRODUCED.

CA S 1074
2026
Covered Provider: Goods and Services: Self Preferencing
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/6/2026
Author: Wiener (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits a covered provider from preferencing its own products, services, or lines of business over those of another business user, including manipulating the order of search results or rankings to favor the products or services of the covered provider. Prohibits a covered provider from restricting interoperability or data portability, including restricting a business user or consumer from obtaining a copy of their data in a useful and portable format. Provides for civil or criminal penalties.
History: Click for History

CA S 1111
2026
Digital Replicas
Status: Pending - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/23/2026
Author: Ashby (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Judicial Use
Summary: Provides that existing law creates a civil cause of action against any person who knowingly uses the name, voice, signature, photograph, or likeness of another person, without their consent, for specified purposes. Clarifies that a voice or likeness includes a digital replica. Clarifies that false impersonation includes the use of a digital replica with the intent to impersonate another.
History: Click for History

CA S 1119
2026
Companion Chatbots: Childrens Safety
Status: Pending - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Padilla (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Personhood
Summary: Requires an operator of a companion chatbot to do various things with respect to child safety and companion chatbots, including annually performing and documenting a comprehensive risk assessment to identify any child safety risk posed by the design, configuration, and operation of the companion chatbot that assesses, among other things, the likelihood of a covered harm occurring to users.
History: Click for History

02/26/2026 - To SENATE Committee on RULES.
03/25/2026 - From SENATE Committee on RULES with author's amendments.
03/25/2026 - In SENATE. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on RULES.

CA S 1133
2026
Pupil Instruction: Preventative Health Instruction
Status: Pending - Senate Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/23/2026
Author: Strickland (R)
Topics: Education Use, Health Use, Studies
Summary: Provides for the Ready to Learn, Ready for Health Act. Requires, when the Health Education Framework for California Public Schools is next revised on or after the specified date, the Instructional Quality Commission to consider including information on evidence based preventative health instruction, including information on nutrition, food literacy, sleep, movement, stress management, and digital balance in an integrated manner that discusses these key health factors and the interrelationship between them.
History: Click for History
02/17/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/26/2026 - To SENATE Committee on EDUCATION.
03/23/2026 - From SENATE Committee on EDUCATION with author's amendments.

CA S 1142
2026
Digital Dignity Act
Status: Pending - Senate Second Reading File
Date of Last Action: 3/23/2026
Author: Becker (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Enacts the Digital Dignity Act. Specifies liability for a person who, by distributing content with actual knowledge that the content includes the use of a digital replica, violates criminal provisions in which false impersonation is a required element or is found liable for defamation in a civil action. Requires a website or application that is a generative AI tool and a large online platform to implement and maintain a mechanism by which users can revoke access to their digital replica.
History: Click for History
02/18/2026 - INTRODUCED.

CA S 1146
2026
Advertisement Claims: Health Related Consumer Products
Status: Pending - Senate Privacy, Digital Technologies, and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Gonzalez (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires, subject to specified exceptions, an advertisement that uses the image, audio, or video of a person representing themselves to be, or identifiably depicting a person as, a health care provider that is generated or substantially altered using artificial intelligence or other computer technology to promote the sale of a health related consumer product or service to include a clear and conspicuous disclosure that the person identifiably depicted is not a health care provider.
History: Click for History

CA S 1159
2026
Artificial Intelligence: Transparency and Governance
Status: Pending - Senate Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Cabaldon (D)
Topics: Government Use, Notification, Personhood
Summary: Specifies that, for purposes of the State Public Records Act, the Bagley Keene Open Meeting Act, the Ralph M. Brown Act, the Political Reform Act of 1974, the Administrative Procedure Act, and the California Environmental Quality Act, the terms person, interested person, participant, member of the public, and any other similar terms under each act referring to those who may engage with governmental agencies, do not include artificial intelligence.

CA A 1159
2026
Student Personal Information
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: Addis (D)
Topics: Education Use, Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Applies the provisions of the K-12 Pupil Online Personal Information Protection Act and the Early Learning Personal Information Protection Act to an operator of a website, online service, online application, or mobile application with actual knowledge that the site, service, or application is used for the applicable school purposes and was designed or marketed for those purposes. Enacts the Higher Education Student Information Protection Act. Authorizes a harmed student or pupil to bring a civil action.

2026

Status: Pending - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Hurtado (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires the Office of Emergency Services, in consultation with the State Department of Education, to establish the Central Valley School Safety Coordination Pilot Program for the purpose of studying and evaluating improved communication pathways between local educational agencies and regional fusion centers regarding credible safety threats affecting school communities.

CA S 1217
2026
Nonconsensual Intimate Image Clearinghouse
Status: Pending - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/24/2026
Author: Grove (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Requires the Department of Justice to establish the Nonconsensual Intimate Image Clearinghouse to allow individuals who were exploited in the State to submit a request for the removal of nonconsensual intimate images from covered platforms. Requires the department to transmit verified identifiers of images to covered platforms. Requires those platforms to remove verified matches within a specified number of hours of receipt and to prevent the images from being uploaded again. Provides for civil penalties.
History: Click for History

2026

Date of Last Action: 2/19/2026
Author: Cabaldon (D)

Summary: Imposes certain restrictions on the use of an automated decision system by a state agency to confer services, defined as, among other things, the issuance of professional licenses and provision of public benefits.

2026
Crimes: Sexual Exploitation of a Child
Status: Pending - Senate Public Safety Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/20/2026
Author: Rubio (D)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Makes a person guilty of a misdemeanor or a felony if the person downloads, streams, or accesses through electronic or digital media material that depicts a person under 18 years of age engaged in an act of sexual conduct, except as specified.

CA A 1609
2026
Customer Service Chatbots
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Zbur (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits an operator of a customer service chatbot available to a person in the State from representing that a customer service chatbot is a human. Requires the operator to provide certain disclosures if a reasonable person interacting with the chatbot would be misled to believe they are interacting with a human. Requires an operator to establish and maintain a process for complaints regarding the customers inability to obtain assistance from a customer service agent.
History: Click for History
01/20/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/19/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION and JUDICIARY.
03/19/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION with author's amendments.
03/19/2026 - In ASSEMBLY. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.

CA A 1651
2026
State Bar of California: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Dixon (R)
Topics: Judicial Use, Notification
Summary: Requires the State Bar to disclose, on its internet website, the use of artificial intelligence generated content in developing or administering State Bar examinations. Requires the State Bar to disclose, on the cover page of study materials, the use of artificial intelligence generated content in examination study material it, among other things, publishes or endorses.
History: Click for History
01/28/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/17/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on JUDICIARY and PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/10/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on JUDICIARY: Do pass to Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/26/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION with author's amendments.
03/26/2026 - In ASSEMBLY. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to ASSEMBLY Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.

CA A 1705
2026
Pornographic Internet Websites
Status: Pending - Assembly Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Bauer-Kahan (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Requires an operator of a pornographic internet website, among other things, to exercise ordinary care and reasonable diligence to ensure that each instance of sexually explicit content displayed on the operator's pornographic internet website does not include a depicted individual. Defines terms, including depicted person, for its purposes.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/23/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION and JUDICIARY.
03/25/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION: Do pass to Committee on JUDICIARY.

CA A 1766
2026
Health Curriculum Framework: Human Trafficking
Status: Pending - Assembly Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/9/2026
Author: Krell (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Education Use
Summary: Requires the Instructional Quality Commission, when the health curriculum framework is next revised on or after January 1, 2027, to consider providing for inclusion in that framework recommendations related to school districts, county offices of education, and charter schools providing annual, developmentally appropriate lessons for each grade served by the local educational agency about how to prevent human trafficking and how to prevent exploitation for labor and services.
History: Click for History
02/09/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/23/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on EDUCATION.
03/18/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on EDUCATION: Do pass to Committee on APPROPRIATIONS.

CA A 1792
2026
Pupil Instruction: Health Framework: Sexual Health
Status: Pending - Assembly Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Rodriguez Mi (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Education Use
Summary: Requires the Instructional Quality Commission, during the next revision of the publication �Health Framework for California Public Schools,� to consider including, and recommending for adoption by the State Board of Education, specific content related to sexual health instruction to educate pupils about dating abuse and digital violence.
History: Click for History
02/10/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/23/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on EDUCATION.
03/18/2026 - From ASSEMBLY Committee on EDUCATION: Do pass to Committee on APPROPRIATIONS.

CA A 1898
2026
Workplace Artificial Intelligence Tools
Status: Pending - Assembly Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/20/2026
Author: Schultz (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Requires an employer to provide a written notice to a worker that a workplace artificial intelligence tool was used to assist the employer in making employment related decisions or to surveil workers in the workplace. Requires the notice to be given to a worker within a specified time and requires the notice to contain specified information, including the specific employment related decisions likely to be affected by the use of the workplace AI tool. Provides for civil actions for damages.
History: Click for History
02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.

CA A 1979
2026
Health Care Services: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Bonta M (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that the Confidentiality of Medical Information Act makes a business that offers software or hardware to consumers for purposes of allowing the individual to manage the individuals information or for the diagnosis, treatment, or management of a medical condition of the individual a provider of health care subject to the requirements of the CMIA. Clarifies that managing the individuals information includes the ability to query their medical history, summarize doctors notes, or organize lab results.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/16/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on HEALTH and PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.

03/19/2026 - In ASSEMBLY. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on HEALTH.

CA A 1988
2026
Companion Chatbots: Crisis Interruption Pauses
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Pellerin (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires, if a companion chatbot detects a credible crisis expression, the companion chatbot to take certain actions, including encouraging the user to seek immediate human support, and, if the companion chatbot detects that a user is reaffirming or escalating the credible crisis expression or detects a subsequent credible crisis expression, require the companion chatbot to initiate a crisis interruption pause of 20 minutes.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/09/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION and HEALTH.

CA A 2023
2026
Companion Chatbots: Childrens Safety
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Wicks (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Right of Action
Summary: Requires an operator of a companion chatbot to do various things with respect to child safety and companion chatbots, including annually perform and document a comprehensive risk assessment to identify any child safety risk posed by the design, configuration, and operation of the companion chatbot that assesses, among other things, the likelihood of a covered harm occurring to users. Requires an operator to submit to an independent audit of its compliance with those provisions.
History: Click for History
02/17/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/19/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committees on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION and JUDICIARY.

CA A 2025
2026
Tenancy: Digitally Altered Images: Disclosure
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/17/2026
Author: Pellerin (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Housing
Summary: Requires a person who includes a digitally altered image, as defined, in an advertisement or other promotional material for the rental of real property to include a disclosure, as specified.
History: Click for History
02/17/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/16/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on PRIVACY AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.

CA A 2027
2026
Worker Data: Prohibitions: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor and Employment Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/16/2026
Author: Ward (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Requires an employer, or a vendor acting on behalf of an employer, to collect and process worker data only as strictly necessary to administer the employment relationship and fulfill specific obligations. Prohibits an employer or vendor from using worker data to train or deploy artificial intelligence to, among other things, replicate, automate, or replace a workers job. Provides for civil actions, damages, injunctive relief, and attorneys fees for violations.
History: Click for History

03/16/2026 - In ASSEMBLY. Read second time and amended. Re-referred to Committee on LABOR AND EMPLOYMENT.

CA A 2148
2026
Local Educational Agency Employees: Public
Status: Pending - Assembly Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Muratsuchi (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Prohibits a certificated or classified employee of a local educational agency or an academic or classified employee of a segment of public postsecondary education from being dismissed, suspended, disciplined, reassigned, transferred, or otherwise retaliated against for, among other things, refusing to use, refusing to deploy, or refusing to direct students to any form of educational technology.

CA A 2169
2026
Social Media Platforms: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/18/2026
Author: Lowenthal (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Enacts the Digital Choice Act. Requires a social media company or a person that deploys an artificial intelligence model, defined as a model operator, to allow a consumer to request a copy of the consumer's personal information, contextual data, and social graph and would require the social media company or model operator to respond to that request within five business days in a format that is, among other things, portable to the extent technically feasible.
History: Click for History

2026
Postsecondary Education: Sexual Harassment

Date of Last Action: 4/6/2026
Author: Bauer-Kahan (D)

Summary: Defines additional terms for purposes of the Equity in Higher Education Act, including, among other terms, �digitized sexually explicit material,� �technology-facilitated sexual harassment,� �affirmative consent,� and �written consent.� The bill would also revise the definitions of �sexual harassment� and �sexual exploitation"".

CA A 2291
2026
Pupil Safety: Nonconsensual Intimate Images: Study
Status: Pending - Assembly Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/6/2026
Author: Lackey (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Education Use
Summary: Requires the State Department of Education to conduct a study on nonconsensual intimate images involving pupils in the specified grades and requires the study to include, among other things, a summary of the resources for schools regarding nonconsensual intimate images and the effects of nonconsensual intimate images on pupils. Requires the department to complete and submit the study to the appropriate fiscal and policy committees of the Legislature.

2026
Public Postsecondary Education: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Higher Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/16/2026
Author: Fong M (D)
Topics: Education/Training, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires the California Community Colleges and the California State University, and requests the University of California, before providing an artificial intelligence product to students, faculty, or staff, to provide a training to students, faculty, or staff on the use of the product.

CA A 2403
2026
Income Tax: Credits: Commercial Production
Status: Pending - Assembly Revenue and Taxation Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Elhawary (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Private Sector Use, Taxes
Summary: Provides that the Personal Income Tax Law and the Corporation Tax Law allow various credits against the taxes imposed by those laws, including numerous motion picture credits. Allows a credit against the taxes imposed by those laws to a qualified taxpayer that produces qualified commercials in the State in an amount equal to specified percentages of the qualified production costs attributable to the production of a qualified commercial.

CA A 2431
2026
Downcoding Medical Claims
Status: Pending - Assembly Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/20/2026
Author: Patel (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits a health care service plan or an insurer issuing group or individual policies of health insurance from using an automated process, system, or tool to downcode a claim, which is the unilateral alteration by a payer of the service or procedure code submitted on a claim resulting in a lower payment. Sets forth requirements for and limitations of downcoding decisions.

CA A 2487
2026
Artificial Intelligence: Education and Workforce
Status: Pending - Assembly Higher Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Macedo (R)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training, Effect on Labor/Employment
Summary: Enacts the Artificial Intelligence Education and Workforce Development Act to accomplish specified goals, including, among others, empowering and supporting community college instructors in using and deploying artificial intelligence responsibly in administrative and classroom settings, and strengthening the States AI and technology workforce pipeline by upskilling and educating all workers so they can benefit from AI.

CA A 2504
2026
Community Colleges: Artificial Intelligence: Employment
Status: Pending - Assembly Higher Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/6/2026
Author: Bauer-Kahan (D)
Topics: Education/Training, Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use, Studies
Summary: Establishes a pilot program for the purpose of addressing employment dislocations associated with artificial intelligence at the California Community Colleges. Requires the chancellors office to submit a report to the Legislature evaluating the effectiveness of the pilot program. Requires the chancellors office to establish an Artificial Intelligence Upskilling Creatives Certificate Program, select a specified number of community college districts to participate in the pilot program, and submit a report.

2026
Report: Labor Force Impact: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor and Employment Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Schiavo (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Studies
Summary: Establishes the State Artificial Intelligence Worker Impact Data Assessment Project and establishes the State Artificial Intelligence Worker Impact Data Assessment Project Advisory Panel in the Employment Development Department. Requires the EDD, in consultation with the advisory panel, to perform an assessment of data sources and collection methods regarding the use and impact of advanced artificial intelligence systems on the labor force.

CA A 2575
2026
Health Care Services: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/18/2026
Author: Ortega (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits a defendant who developed, modified, or used artificial intelligence or a clinical decision support system that is alleged to have harmed the plaintiff from asserting a defense that the failure of a licensed health care professional or other health care worker to override an output of the artificial intelligence or clinical decision support system is a superseding cause severing the defendants liability for the alleged harm.

2026
Crimes: Prostitution

Author: Schultz (D)

Summary: Requires a person who commits prostitution with intent to receive compensation, money, or anything of value from another person to, for a first or second violation of those provisions, be offered a diversion program pursuant to specified provisions.

2026

Author: Lee (D)

Summary: Requires the Department of Industrial Relations to update its contractor responsibility program to include a Sweatfree AI Code of Conduct to be signed by all bidders on State contracts and subcontracts, similar to the Sweatfree Code of Conduct, and solicit input from an advisory panel for that purpose. Requires the updated contractor responsibility program and Sweatfree AI Code of Conduct to include specified information related to AI products.

2026
Public Employees: Notice: Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Petrie-Norris (D)
Topics: Government Use, Notification
Summary: Relates to the deployment of generative artificial intelligence tools by the State. Requires certain State and local public employers to provide written notice to a recognized employee organization at least a specified number of days before taking an action to develop, purchase, implement, or utilize GenAI to perform a service that is within the scope of work of the job classification represented by the recognized employee organization.

CA A 2713
2026
California AI Transparency Act: Civil Penalty
Status: Pending - Assembly Privacy and Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/6/2026
Author: Wicks (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that the State AI Transparency Act requires, among other things, a person that creates, codes, or otherwise produces a generative artificial intelligence system that has over a specified number of monthly visitors or users and is publicly accessible within the geographic boundaries of the State to make available an AI detection tool at no cost to the user. Provides that a violator of such provisions is liable for a civil penalty. Increases the civil penalty to a specified amount per violation.

CO H 1139
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence in Health Care
Status: Pending - HOUSE
Date of Last Action: 3/13/2026
Author: Joseph (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires entities that use an artificial intelligence system or algorithm for utilization review to ensure that the system complies with certain requirements, relates to the unauthorized practice of psychotherapy, requires disclosure of a clients right to consent to a disclosure of confidential communications, prohibits a health insurance carrier and a payer of services under the Medical Assistance Act and the Childrens Basic Health Plan Act from paying for psychotherapy services conducted by an AI system.

CO H 1210
2026
Unfair or Deceptive Trade Practices
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Bacon (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits discrimination against a consumer or worker through the use of automated decision systems used to engage in individualized price setting based on surveillance data regarding a consumer, prohibits individualized wage setting based on such data regarding a worker, specifies activities that are not prohibited as individualized price or wage setting based on surveillance data regarding a consumer or worker, provides that the Attorney General or a district attorney may bring certain civil actions.

Connecticut
CT S 4
2026
Consumer Privacy
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 4/1/2026
Author: Looney (D) Additional Authors: Kushner (D);Honig (D);Gadkar-Wilcox (D);Rahman (D);Marx (D);Maher (D);Hochadel (D);Gaston (D);Cabrera (D);Anwar (D);Needleman (D);Hartley (D);Cohen (D);Slap (D);Osten (D);Maroney (D);Lopes (D);Miller P (D);Lesser (D);Flexer (D);McCrory (D);Duff (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Concerns consumer privacy and protection, requires the controller to perform its responsibility to respond to a consumer's rights when the controller processes that consumer's personal data by automated means, applies when such data is used for profiling to make a solely automated decision that results in either providing or denying an employment opportunity, prohibits controller to sell, share or transfer, or allow any other person to access, precise geolocation data.
History: Click for History

Summary: Concerns online safety, establishes various requirements concerning artificial intelligence, artificial intelligence systems, artificial intelligence technologies, artificial intelligence companions and automated employment-related decision processes and an Artificial Intelligence Policy Office to be overseen by an Artificial Intelligence Policy Director, requires subscription-based artificial intelligence providers to make consumer disclosure.

Topics: Health Use

DE H 233
2026
Large Energy Use Facilities
Status: Pending - HOUSE
Date of Last Action: 6/30/2025
Author: Burns (D) Additional Authors: Heffernan (D);Hansen (D);Phillips (D);Romer (D);Gorman (D)
Topics: Energy, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires regulated utilities to establish a separate rate for large energy-use facilities that mitigates the risk of costs associated with expanding infrastructure and maintaining reliability in the face of growing demand from being shifted to residential, small business, and other electric customers.

DE H 306
2026
Communication with a Computer
Status: Pending - HOUSE
Date of Last Action: 3/5/2026
Author: Romer (D) Additional Authors: Sokola (D);Townsend (D);Walsh (D);Griffith (D);Morrison (D);Gorman (D);Berry (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Makes it an unlawful practice to engage in a commercial transaction with a consumer who interacts with computer technology, under circumstances where a reasonable person would believe that person is engaging with an actual human, without notifying the consumer that the consumer is communicating with a computer and not a human being.

FL S 482
2026

Status: Failed - Adjourned - HOUSE
Date of Last Action: 3/4/2026
Author: Leek (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Government Use, Notification
Associated Bills: FL H 659 - Compare;FL H 1395 - Similar;FL S 7030 - Linked
Summary: Relates to the Artificial Intelligence Bill of Rights, defines the term artificial intelligence, prohibits a governmental entity from extending or renewing a contract with specified entities, beginning on a specified date, prohibits a local governmental entity from taking certain actions relating to contracting with an entity to provide artificial intelligence technology, software, or products unless certain requirements are met, beginning on a specified date.

FL H 527
2026
Mandatory Human Reviews of Insurance Claim Denials
Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Cassel (D) Additional Authors: Salzman (R);Cross (D);Robinson Fe (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: FL S 202 - Compare
Summary: Requires that insurers decisions to deny claims or any portion of claim be made by qualified human professionals, provides duties of qualified human professionals before they make certain determinations, requires insurers to maintain certain records, prohibits algorithms, artificial intelligence systems, and machine learning systems from being used as sole basis for determining whether to adjust or deny claim or portion of claim.

2026

Author: Hunschofsky (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use

Summary: Requires operators of companion chatbot platforms to display specified information on any application, browser, or format used to access companion chatbots on platform and to prevent such chatbots from engaging with users unless specified protocols are maintained, requires such operators to verify age of users and to take specified actions for users who are minors, requires such operators to file specified reports with Department of Legal Affairs (DLA).

FL H 905
2026

Status: To Governor - To Enrollment
Date of Last Action: 3/12/2026
Author: Persons (R) Additional Authors: Robinson (R);Valdes (D);Fabricio (R);Rizo (R);Trabulsy (R);Alvarez (R);Cassel (D);Holcomb (R);Redondo (R);Miller M (R);Owen (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections, Notification
Associated Bills: FL S 1178 - Similar
Summary: Defines the terms ""designated foreign terrorist organization"" and ""foreign country of concern"", provides penalties for specified persons who solicit or accept anything of value from persons or entities representing a designated foreign terrorist organization or a foreign country of concern, requires the Commission on Ethics to adopt certain rules by a specified date, authorizes any appropriate tax collector to revoke or refuse to renew business tax receipts of specified individuals, businesses, or entity.

FL S 1062
2026
Speech and Debate
Status: Failed - Adjourned - Concurrence
Date of Last Action: 3/11/2026
Author: Brodeur (R) Additional Authors: Garcia (R);Polsky (D);Rodriguez (R);Pizzo (D);Wright (R);Arrington (D);Truenow (R);Bradley J (R);Burgess (R);DiCeglie (R);Osgood (D);Calatayud (R);Martin (R);Simon (R);Mayfield (R);Gaetz (R);Guillermo Smith (D);Sharief (D);Burton (R);Hooper (R);Rouson (D);Bernard (D);Albritton (R);Berman (D);Boyd (R);Passidomo (R);Jones (D);Harrell (R);Trumbull (R);Davis (D);Grall (R);Gruters (R);Leek (R);Massullo (R);McClain (R);Yarborough (R)
Topics: Education Use
Associated Bills: FL H 1059 - Compare
Summary: Relates to speech and debate education, creates the State Speech and Debate Hall of Fame, requires the Department of Management Services to set aside an area in the Capitol Building for the hall of fame, requires the department to consult with the State Education Foundation and the Commissioner of Education, provides the duties for the hall of fame, requiring the hall of fame to convene a committee for specified purposes, designates State Speech and Debate Week annually in February.

FL H 1159
2026
Sexual Offenses
Status: Enacted - Act No. 2026-20
Date of Last Action: 03/31/2026 - Enacted
Author: Jacques (R) Additional Authors: Daniels (D);Mooney (R);Baker (R);Gossett-Seidman (R);Lopez (D);Steele (R);Yarkosky (R);Nix (R);Weinberger (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Associated Bills: FL H 245 - Compare;FL S 398 - Compare;FL S 1750 - Compare
Summary: Replaces the term ""child pornography"" with the term ""child sexual abuse material, replaces the term ""child pornography"" with the term ""child sexual abuse material"", revises critera related to the reclassification of specified offenses, revises mandatory minimum sentences for certain sexual offenses by persons previously convicted of sexual offenses.

2026

Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Grall (R) Additional Authors: Avila (R)
Topics: Deepfake
Associated Bills: FL H 905 - Similar
Summary: Requires agents of foreign countries of concern and foreign-supported political organizations to register with the Division of Elections, requires periodic updates by such agents and organizations, defines the terms designated foreign terrorist organization and foreign country of concern, prohibits a governmental entity from entering into certain contracts with certain entities owned or partially owned by foreign sources of concern or affiliated with a foreign country of concern.

2026

Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Education Pre-K-12
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Jones (D)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Relates to artificial intelligence in education, requires the state board of education to adopt statewide standards and policies for artificial intelligence use, requires the board to direct the department to provide specified teacher training and review school district compliance, requires students in specified grade levels to receive instruction on digital literacy and ethics of artificial intelligence use in certain contexts.

Author: Tuck (R) Additional Authors: Partington (R)
Topics: Government Use
Associated Bills: FL S 1080 - Compare;FL S 1310 - Similar
Summary: Requires railroad crossing constructed after specified date to include advanced detection and monitoring system and provides requirements therefor, requires existing railroad crossing upgrade or significant modification occurring after specified date to incorporate such system, requires DOT, in consultation with rail operators and safety experts, to develop certain standards, identify priority existing railroad crossings, and provide technical assistance to rail operators.

FL S 1344
2026
Companion Artificial Intelligence Chatbots
Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Commerce and Tourism Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Burton (R)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: FL H 659 - Compare;FL S 1346 - Linked
Summary: Requires operators to require an individual seeking access to a companion ai chatbot to create a user account, requires operators to take certain actions if the age verification process determines that a user is a minor, requires operators to protect the confidentiality of age verification information provided by a user, provides that violations of the act are deemed deceptive and unfair trade practices.

FL H 1395
2026
Artificial Intelligence

Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Rizo (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Government Use, Notification
Associated Bills: FL H 659 - Compare;FL S 482 - Similar;FL S 7030 - Compare
Summary: Prohibits governmental entities from extending or renewing contract with specified entities, prohibits governmental entities from taking certain actions relating to contracting for AI technology, software, or products unless certain requirements are met, prohibits governmental entities from entering into contracts for AI technology, software, or products under certain conditions, provides rights of Floridians relating to ai use, requires consent for minors to create new or maintain existing companion chat.

GA H 7
2026
Income Tax
Status: Pending - HOUSE
Date of Last Action: 2/26/2026
Author: Kendrick (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Taxes
Summary: Relates to income taxes, provides for a tax credit for workforce ready graduates employed in high tech full time jobs in rural counties in the State, requires the Department of Labor to establish certain criteria, provides for conditions and limitations, provides that no individual shall be allowed more than a specified amount of tax credits, provides that no individual first employed in a high tech full time job before the specified date shall qualify to be eligible to receive the credit.

2026

GA S 398
2026
Virtual Peeping on an Adult
Status: Pending - Concurrence
Date of Last Action: 3/27/2026
Author: Hatchett (R) Additional Authors: Kirkpatrick (R);Dickerson J (R);Echols Dr (R);Still (R);Hodges (R);Goodman (R);Dixon (R);Anavitarte (R);Wade (R);Hickman (R);Summers (R);Robertson (R);Burns (R);Payne (R);Brass (R);Harbin (R);Watson S (R);Strickland (R);Albers (R);Anderson (R);Cowsert (R);Setzler (R);Bearden (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Relates to peeping toms, establishes a criminal offense of virtual peeping on an adult, provides that a person commits the offense of virtual peeping on an adult when such person knowingly and intentionally causes a generative artificial intelligence system to generate obscene material that includes an image of an adult with the knowledge that the generation of such material including such image was not authorized or consented to by such adult.

2026

Summary: Relates to commissions and other agencies, so as to provide for the establishment of the Georgia Artificial Intelligence Commission, provides for responsibilities, provides for purpose, provides for assignment to the Georgia Technology Authority, provides for annual reports, provides for membership, terms, meetings, and compensation, provides for definitions, provides for related matters, repeals conflicting laws.

GA S 488
2026

Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Setzler (R) Additional Authors: Harrell (D);Parent (D);Strickland (R);Harbin (R)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Relates to general provisions relative to torts, so as to provide that generative artificial intelligence systems shall constitute personal property for purposes of certain actions for product liability alleging injury to a minor, provides for liability of product sellers in such actions, establishes rebuttable presumptions relative to manufacturers and product sellers in such actions, provides for definitions, provides for an effective date and applicability, provides for related matters.

Author: Parkes (D) Additional Authors: Orrock (D);Parent (D);Anderson T (D);Mallow (D);Jackson (D);Merritt (D);Mangham (D)

GA S 540
2026
Online Internet Safety
Status: To Governor - Eligible for Governor
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Anavitarte (R) Additional Authors: Bearden (R);Watson B (R);Strickland (R);Harbin (R);Walker (R);Jones T (R);Still (R);Dickerson J (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Relates to online internet safety, requires certain disclosures related to AI companion chatbots, requires the operators of AI companion chatbots to perform age verification in certain circumstances, provides for AI companion chatbots operated by licensed mental health, behavioral health, medical, or counseling professionals, provides for certain privacy tools, requires operators to adopt a protocol for an AI companion chatbot's response to suicidal ideation or self-harm.

GA S 559
2026
Fair Business Practices Act
Status: Pending - Senate Regulated Industries and Utilities Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/24/2026
Author: Rahman (D) Additional Authors: Rhett (D);Howard Ja (D);Wicks (D);Mangham (D);Kemp (D);Parkes (D);Halpern (D);Merritt (D);Jackson (D);Mallow (D);McLaurin (D);Lucas (D);Jones (D);Anderson T (D);Parent (D);Sims F (D);Davenport (D);Jones E (D);James (D);Harbison (D);Harrell (D);Orrock (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relates to the Fair Business Practices Act of 1975, so as to prohibit any person from facilitating noncompete agreements between residential rental property owners or managers, including by use of algorithmic coordinating functions, prohibits rental property owners or managers from setting or adjusting rental prices, lease renewal terms, occupancy levels, or other lease terms and conditions based on recommendations from software, data analytics service, or algorithmic devices.
History: Click for History
02/24/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/25/2026 - To SENATE Committee on REGULATED INDUSTRIES and UTILITIES.

2026

Date of Last Action: 2/25/2026
Author: Hong (R) Additional Authors: Frye (D);Jones T (R);Carpenter (R);Gunter (R);Bell (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relating to selling and other trade practices, so as to enact the NO FAKES Act of 2026, protects intellectual property rights in the voice and visual likeness of individuals, provides for the characteristics of a right to use the voice or visual likeness in a digital replica, provides for licensing and transferability of a right, requires the Secretary of State to maintain a directory of certain post-mortem digital replication rights and a directory of designated agents.

HI H 1598
2026
University Climate Institute
Status: Pending - House Energy and Environmental Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Garrett (D) Additional Authors: Woodson (D);Kapela (D);Sayama (D);Amato (D);Kila (D);Souza (R);Evslin (D);La Chica (D);Olds (D)
Topics: Education Use
Associated Bills: HI S 2484 - Companion
Summary: Establishes the Hawaii Climate Institute at the University of Hawaii to support climate resilience and adaptation initiatives of the University and the State, requires a percentage of the revenue generated by any increase to the Transient Accommodations Tax rates beginning on January 1, 2026, and by assessment of the Transient Accommodations Tax on gross rental proceeds derived from cruise fares to be allocated to the University of Hawaii annually to support the programs and operations.

HI S 1618
2026
Journalistic Ethics Commission
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2025
Author: Kim (D) Additional Authors: Wakai (D);Dela Cruz (D);San Buenaventur (D);DeCoite (D);Hashimoto (D);Fevella (R);Richards (D)
Topics: Studies
Summary: Establishes a Journalistic Ethics Commission within the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, establishes a Journalistic Code of Ethics, requires news media outlets to provide training in the ethics of journalism to journalists and editors in their employ, establishes a Journalistic Ethics Review Board to be administratively attached to the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs, requires the Journalistic Ethics Review Board to handle complaints and appeals regarding violations.

HI H 1787
2026
Health Care Software Restrictions
Status: Pending - House Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: La Chica (D) Additional Authors: Quinlan (D);Perruso (D);Ilagan (D);Amato (D);Garcia (R);Garrett (D);Kahaloa (D);Kila (D);Poepoe (D);Souza (R);Kusch (D);Grandinetti (D);Hussey (D);Iwamoto (D);Olds (D);Shimizu (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Associated Bills: HI S 2768 - Companion
Summary: Establishes restrictions on the use of artificial intelligence, algorithms, or other software tools for purposes of decision making in health insurance utilization reviews, requires a licensed health care provider to review all adverse actions by the health carrier.

HI H 1887
2026
Statewide Artificial Intelligence Literacy Curricula
Status: Pending - House Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Woodson (D) Additional Authors: Sayama (D);Olds (D);Hussey (D);Grandinetti (D);Kusch (D);Souza (R);Poepoe (D);Kila (D);Garrett (D);Amato (D);Tam (D);Belatti (D);Marten (D);Ilagan (D);Tarnas (D);Perruso (D);Matayoshi (D);Todd (D);Takayama (D);Matsumoto (R);Morikawa (D);Lee C (D)
Topics: Appropriations, Education Use, Education/Training, Studies
Associated Bills: HI S 2212 - Companion
Summary: Requires the Department of Education to develop and implement a statewide artificial intelligence literacy curricula plan for students in eleventh and twelfth grades, beginning with the 2027-2028 school year as a condition for graduation, establishes the advisory council for artificial intelligence education, establishes a three year artificial intelligence education grant pilot program within the Department of Education, requires reports to the Legislature, appropriates funds.
History: Click for History

HI H 2137
2026

Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 3/30/2026
Author: Lee M (D) Additional Authors: Amato (D);Gedeon (R);Templo (D);Hussey (D);Grandinetti (D);Kusch (D);Miyake (D);Souza (R);Poepoe (D);Kila (D);Kahaloa (D);Chun (D);Belatti (D);Sayama (D);Marten (D);Ilagan (D);Tarnas (D);Perruso (D);Woodson (D);Takayama (D);Lowen (D);Matsumoto (R);Lee C (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits certain harmful uses of and mandates disclosure for realistic digital imitations generated by artificial intelligence, establishes certain exemptions, provides for civil actions and civil remedies for individuals injured by unauthorized AI-generated realistic digital imitations, requires the disclosure of the use of synthetic performers in advertising, establishes civil fines.

2026

Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Richards (D) Additional Authors: Fukunaga (D);McKelvey (D);San Buenaventur (D);Chang (D);Hashimoto (D)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Revises the state Patients' Bill of Rights and responsibilities act by establishing new provisions on telehealth parity, prior authorization timelines, and automated decision systems, enhancing medical data protection and privacy standards, expanding the Insurance Commissioner's enforcement authority, and improving network adequacy, internal and external appeals procedures, and reporting requirements.

Topics: Appropriations, Education Use, Education/Training, Oversight/Governance, Studies

Summary: Requires the Department of Education to develop and implement a statewide artificial intelligence literacy curricula plan for students in eleventh and twelfth grades, beginning with the 2027-2028 school year as a condition for graduation, establishes the advisory council for artificial intelligence education, establishes a three year artificial intelligence education grant pilot program within the Department of Education, requires reports to the Legislature, appropriates funds.

HI S 2281
2026
Patient Interactions Artificial Intelligence Use
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 2/18/2026
Author: San Buenaventur (D) Additional Authors: Fukunaga (D);McKelvey (D);Rhoads (D);Kidani (D);Keohokalole (D);Chang (D);Hashimoto (D);Fevella (R);Moriwaki (D);Richards (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Requires health care providers using artificial intelligence (AI) in patient interactions to disclose to the patient that the patient is interacting with artificial intelligence, requires health care providers using ai in making consequential decisions relating to the patient to provide certain notice and statements to the patient, maintains a qualified AI oversight personnel who shall be a natural person that reviews, evaluates, and validates or overrides AI outputs.
History: Click for History
01/15/2026 - PREFILED.
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.

2026
Identity Theft

Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Kouchi (D) Additional Authors: Nakamura (D);Tarnas (D);Ilagan (D);Poepoe (D);Hussey (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake

2026

Status: Pending - Senate Ways and Means Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Nakamura (D)
Topics: Health Use, Oversight/Governance
Associated Bills: HI S 3139 - Companion
Summary: Amends the functions and responsibilities of the State Health Planning and Development Agency, adds a new definition of health care, amends the exemption threshold for bed changes to up to thirty per cent of existing licensed bed types, exempts the Department of Health from certificate of need requirements.

HI H 2357
2026
Artificial Intelligence Use
Status: Pending - House Judiciary & Hawaiian Affairs Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/11/2026
Author: Ilagan (D) Additional Authors: Kapela (D);Marten (D);Amato (D);Kila (D);Poepoe (D);Souza (R);Kusch (D);Grandinetti (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits music streaming platforms from hosting, distributing, or otherwise making available in the State music performed or attributed to an artificial intelligence music artist, makes violations an unfair method of competition and an unfair and deceptive act or practice.

HI H 2379
2026
Insurance Fraud
Status: Pending - House Consumer Protection and Commerce Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Hashem (D) Additional Authors: Matayoshi (D);Ilagan (D);Marten (D)
Topics: Government Use, Health Use
Associated Bills: HI S 2948 - Companion
Summary: Increases the scope of insurance fraud and certain offenses, augments offenses by contractors and insurers during a declared emergency, clarifies the penalties for the offense of insurance fraud and the capabilities and operations of the insurance fraud investigations branch, including the annual report to the Legislature and the protection of sensitive information, adds protection for insurance fraud whistleblowers, authorizes the Insurance Commissioner to establish a centralized database.

Summary: Requires the Office of Consumer Protection of the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs to conduct a study to determine the extent to which companies operating in the State deploy surveillance pricing practices, requires a report to the Legislature, appropriates funds.

HI H 2466
2026
Schools Phone Use and Social Media Education
Status: Pending - House Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Gedeon (R) Additional Authors: Matsumoto (R);Garcia (R);Kahaloa (D);Kila (D);Pierick (R);Poepoe (D);Lee Loy (D);Muraoka (R);Shimizu (R)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Requires the Department of Education to prohibit electronic telecommunication device usage during the instructional day and implement a social media and artificial intelligence literacy education campaign, creates exemptions for students requiring accommodations, emergency situations, and teacher authorizations, requires the Department of Education to submit a report three years after the initial effective date, and then every five years thereafter.
History: Click for History
01/27/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/02/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on EDUCATION.
02/02/2026 - Subsequent referral set for: HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY & HAWAIIAN AFFAIRS.

HI S 2484
2026
Climate Resilience and Adaptation Initiatives
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Gabbard (D) Additional Authors: Woodson (D);Kapela (D);Sayama (D);Amato (D);Garrett (D);Kila (D);Souza (R);Evslin (D);La Chica (D);Olds (D)
Topics: Education Use
Associated Bills: HI H 1598 - Companion
Summary: Establishes the Hawaii Climate Institute at the University of Hawaii to support climate resilience and adaptation initiatives of the university and the state, requires six percent of the revenue generated by any increase to the transient accommodations tax rates beginning on January 1, 2026, and by assessment of the transient accommodations tax on gross rental proceeds derived from cruise fares to be allocated to the University of Hawaii annually to support the programs and operations.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/28/2026 - To SENATE Committee on EDUCATION.

HI H 2499
2026
Demographic Data
Status: Pending - House Economic Development & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Hartsfield (D) Additional Authors: Belatti (D);Lowen (D);Matayoshi (D);Perruso (D);Marten (D);Tam (D);Amato (D);Chun (D);Kila (D);Poepoe (D);Souza (R);Miyake (D);Grandinetti (D);Lee Loy (D);Templo (D)
Topics: Government Use, Oversight/Governance, Responsible Use
Summary: Requires the Office of Enterprise Technology Services to coordinate the development and maintenance of statewide standards for the collection, management, and reporting of race and ethnicity demographic data by any state or county department, agency, board, or commission, focuses the use of collected demographic data on areas of public interest and establishes transparency and accountability requirements regarding artificial intelligence systems associated with the collected data.
History: Click for History
01/28/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/02/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT & TECHNOLOGY.

02/06/2026 - In Committee: Measure Deferred.

HI H 2500
2026
Artificial Intelligence Disclosures
Status: Pending - House Economic Development & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Hartsfield (D) Additional Authors: Belatti (D);Lowen (D);Marten (D);Amato (D);Chun (D);Kila (D);Pierick (R);Poepoe (D);Souza (R);Miyake (D);Kusch (D);Grandinetti (D);Hussey (D);Iwamoto (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires, beginning January 1, 2027, a developer of an algorithmic decision system to provide certain disclosures to a deployer and an individual who is or will be affected by a decision made, informed, or influenced by the algorithmic decision system, provides certain rights and procedures for individual's to access and correct data used by an algorithmic decision system, requires disclosure requirements for generative artificial intelligence systems.
History: Click for History
01/28/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/02/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT & TECHNOLOGY.

HI H 2502
2026
Artificial Intelligence Disclosures
Status: Pending - House Consumer Protection and Commerce Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/11/2026
Author: Matayoshi (D) Additional Authors: Cochran (D);Templo (D);Lee Loy (D);Iwamoto (D);Grandinetti (D);Kusch (D);Miyake (D);Takenouchi (D);Poepoe (D);Kila (D);Garrett (D);Belatti (D);Amato (D);Marten (D);Tarnas (D);Perruso (D);Holt (D);Takayama (D);Lowen (D);Morikawa (D);Lee C (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: HI S 3001 - Companion
Summary: Requires operators of conversational artificial intelligence services in the State to issue certain disclosures to account holders and users, requires operators to develop protocols to respond to prompts or content associated with, and prevent the production of, suicidal ideations in account holders and users, establishes protections for minor account holders of conversational artificial intelligence services, requires operators to submit annual reports to the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs.

2026

Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: HI S 3027 - Companion
Summary: Establishes patient rights with respect to timely access to specialists and referrals and prior authorization determination timelines, establishes certain requirements for health carriers for prior authorization determinations, establishes certain requirements for the use of automated decision support tools for claims determinations and utilization review, requires health carriers to establish certain safeguards for protected health information, establishes certain reporting requirements for network.

2026
Film and Creative Industries Development Special Fund
Status: Pending - House Finance Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: DeCoite (D) Additional Authors: Moriwaki (D)

Summary: Establishes the Hawaii Film Commission within the Department of Business, Economic Development, and Tourism and transfers the rights, powers, functions, and duties of the Hawaii Film Office to the Commission, requires appointments and hiring of staff by specified date, establishes the Film Industry Development Special Fund. Excludes media and film from the definition of creative industries.

2026
Artificial Intelligence Online Clearinghouse
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 1/22/2026
Author: Gabbard (D) Additional Authors: Chang (D)
Topics: Government Use, Health Use
Summary: Requires the Department of Health, in coordination with the Department of Budget and Finance and Office of Enterprise Technology Services, and Department of Human Services, to establish and maintain an online clearinghouse enhanced by artificial intelligence of evidence based treatment and prevention programs, requires Department of Health to support the integration of artificial intelligence in the online clearinghouse.

HI H 2591
2026
State Open Data Portal Website
Status: Pending - House Economic Development & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Quinlan (D) Additional Authors: Garrett (D);Templo (D);Olds (D);Lee Loy (D);Kusch (D);Miyake (D);La Chica (D);Evslin (D);Takenouchi (D);Kila (D);Kahaloa (D);Morikawa (D);Tam (D);Sayama (D);Marten (D);Ilagan (D);Perruso (D);Matayoshi (D);Kitagawa (D);Kong (D);Takayama (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Requires the Chief Data Officer to enhance the state's open data portal website through the phased development and publishing of an artificial intelligence chatbot and necessary data visualizations on statewide shared data sets to assist the public in navigating the open data portal website and finding information about statewide services across state departments and agencies, requires the Chief Data Officer to develop a system to evaluate and select for procurement artificial intelligence governance tool.

Summary: Requires the Chief Data Officer to enhance the state's open data portal website through the phased development and publishing of an artificial intelligence chatbot and necessary data visualizations on statewide shared data sets to assist the public, requires the Chief Data Officer to develop a system to evaluate and select artificial intelligence governance tools for use by state departments and agencies and establish a mechanism for reporting and publication of artificial intelligence use cases.

HI S 2908
2026

Status: Pending - House Water & Land Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/20/2026
Author: Wakai (D) Additional Authors: Amato (D);Templo (D);Olds (D);Lee Loy (D);Kusch (D);Miyake (D);Evslin (D);Takenouchi (D);Kila (D);Kahaloa (D);Belatti (D);Tam (D);Sayama (D);Marten (D);Kapela (D);Ilagan (D);Tarnas (D);Matayoshi (D);Lowen (D);Morikawa (D)
Topics: Government Use
Associated Bills: HI H 1968 - Companion

2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Gabbard (D) Additional Authors: Chang (D)
Topics: Oversight/Governance
Summary: Establishes the Office of Artificial Intelligence Safety and Regulation within the Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs to regulate the development, deployment, and use of artificial intelligence technologies in the state, prohibits the deployment of artificial intelligence products in the state unless affirmative proof establishing the product's safety is submitted to the office, appropriates funds.

2026
Insurance Fraud
Status: Pending - House Finance Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/30/2026
Author: McKelvey (D) Additional Authors: Hashem (D);Matayoshi (D);Ilagan (D);Marten (D)
Topics: Government Use, Health Use
Associated Bills: HI H 2379 - Companion
Summary: Prohibits certain solicitation and inducement practices relating to insurance claims. broadens the scope of the offense of insurance fraud and the acts that constitute insurance fraud, establishes disaster-related insurance fraud offenses, clarifies the penalties for the offense of insurance fraud and the capabilities and operations of the Insurance Fraud Investigations Branch, including the annual report to the Legislature and the use of fraud analytics.

HI S 2953
2026
Artificial Intelligence Protections
Status: Pending - In Multiple Committees
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: McKelvey (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Establishes consumer protections and insurer governance requirements for the use of artificial intelligence systems in certain insurance practices, includes health insurance benefit determinations and residential property underwriting decisions, establishes review, monitoring, recordkeeping, disclosure, rebuttal, and cure requirements for the use of artificial intelligence systems in certain insurance practices, requires the Insurance Commissioner to adopt rules.
History: Click for History
01/23/2026 - INTRODUCED.

HI S 2967
2026
Artificial Intelligence Consumer Protections
Status: Pending - Senate Labor and Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: McKelvey (D) Additional Authors: Chang (D);Hashimoto (D);Richards (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Establishes consumer protection requirements for the use of artificial intelligence systems in consumer interactions and consequential decisions, including disclosures, documentation, and a right to correction, appeal, and human review, makes certain violations an unfair or deceptive act or practice, requires risk management and impact assessments for high risk artificial intelligence systems, requires incident reports to the Executive Director of the Office of Consumer Protection and the Attorney General.
History: Click for History
01/23/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To SENATE Committee on LABOR AND TECHNOLOGY.
01/30/2026 - Subsequent referral set for: SENATE Committee on COMMERCE AND CONSUMER PROTECTION.
02/06/2026 - In Committee: Public Hearing Scheduled.
02/11/2026 - In Committee: Measure Deferred.

2026

Status: Pending - House Judiciary & Hawaiian Affairs Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/30/2026
Author: Keohokalole (D) Additional Authors: Cochran (D);Templo (D);Lee Loy (D);Iwamoto (D);Grandinetti (D);Kusch (D);Miyake (D);Takenouchi (D);Poepoe (D);Kila (D);Garrett (D);Belatti (D);Amato (D);Marten (D);Tarnas (D);Perruso (D);Matayoshi (D);Holt (D);Takayama (D);Lowen (D);Morikawa (D);Lee C (D)

2026

Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: HI H 2537 - Companion
Summary: Establishes patient rights with respect to timely access to specialists and referrals and prior authorization determination timelines, establishes certain requirements for health carriers for prior authorization determinations, establishes certain requirements for the use of automated decision support tools for claims determinations and utilization review, requires health carriers to establish certain safeguards for protected health information, establishes certain reporting requirements.
