03/23/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/24/2026 - To SENATE Committee on STATE AFFAIRS.
03/25/2026 - From SENATE Committee on STATE AFFAIRS: Do pass.

Illinois
IL HJR 50
2026
Evidence-Based Funding Model and AI
Status: Pending - House Appropriations-Elementary & Secondary Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Dias (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Directs the Professional Review Panel and the State Board of Education to conduct the analysis and financial modeling required to evaluate the current, likely, and potential impacts of artificial intelligence and related technologies on the cost factors within the evidence-based funding model, directs PRP and the ISBE to submit their findings in a written report to the General Assembly and Governor by a specified date.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/20/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/11/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on APPROPRIATIONS-ELEMENTARY & SECONDARY EDUCATION.

IL SR 610
2026
Artificial Intelligence in K 12 Classrooms
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Assignments Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Johnson (D)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training, Responsible Use
Summary: Supports the principles put forth by ASCD/PD365 regarding the ethical and effective use of artificial intelligence in K-12 classrooms, urges the State Board of Education , in collaboration with school districts, educators, technology experts, and stakeholder organizations, to develop statewide guidelines for the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence in education, including protections for student data, expectations for algorithmic transparency and monitoring for bias.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.

IL S 1996
2026
Algorithmic Rent Fixing Prevention
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2025
Author: Guzman (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Preventing Algorithmic Rent Fixing in the Rental Housing Market Act, provides that a real estate lessor, or any agent or subcontractor of a real estate lessor, shall not subscribe to, contract with, or otherwise exchange anything of value in return for the services of a real estate service provider, provides that a real estate service provider shall not facilitate an agreement to not compete between real estate lessors with respect to residential dwelling units.
History: Click for History

02/06/2025 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.

IL S 2255
2026
Surveillance Based Price and Wage Discrimination Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/7/2025
Author: Peters (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Creates the Surveillance-Based Price and Wage Discrimination Act, provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized price assessed to a consumer for goods or services, provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized wage paid to an employee, sets forth exemptions to the provisions, provides that the Attorney General shall enforce the Act.

IL S 2780
2026
Department of Central Management Services
Status: Pending - Senate Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Collins (D)
Topics: Appropriations, Government Use
Summary: Appropriates a specified amount from the General Revenue Fund to the Department of Central Management Services for the purpose of making a grant to Amp AI, doing business as Nozma, to conduct a procurement modernization and artificial intelligence pilot program.
History: Click for History
01/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/13/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.
02/03/2026 - To SENATE Committee on APPROPRIATIONS.

IL S 2786
2026
Procurement Code
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Assignments Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Collins (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Amends the Procurement Code, provides that the Department of Central Management Services may enter into a sole source contract with a specified contractor for a 6-month procurement modernization pilot project, provides that the contractor shall conduct a comprehensive procurement modernization study for units of local government, provides that the pilot program shall be conducted in no more than 7 municipalities of the contractor's choosing.
History: Click for History
01/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/13/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.

IL S 2791
2026
Vehicle Code
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Assignments Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Murphy L (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Amends the Vehicle Code, provides that using an electronic communication device includes recording a video, holding a device in one's hand or lap, composing, sending, or reading any text-based communication, or browsing the Internet, provides that a person is presumed to be in use of an electronic communication device while driving if the electronic communication device is observed in the person's hand while the vehicle is in motion or stopped in traffic.
History: Click for History

IL S 2909
2026
Teacher Evaluation
Status: Pending - Senate Third Reading
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Belt (D)
Topics: Education Use, Effect on Labor/Employment
Summary: Amends the Evaluation of Certified Employees Article of the School Code, prohibits an evaluator from using an artificial intelligence tool to assign a numerical score or qualitative rating for any component of a teacher's evaluation or any evaluation task that requires professional judgment, allows an artificial intelligence tool to be used to support the evaluator in administrative tasks.

IL S 2927
2026
Commerce and Economic Opportunity Law
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Simmons (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Studies
Summary: Amends the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity Law of the Civil Administrative Code, provides that the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity shall conduct, in consultation with the Department of Labor, a comprehensive study to identify strategies to protect jobs that are at risk of loss due to artificial intelligence, provides that the study shall include specified recommendations and analysis.
History: Click for History

IL S 2993
2026
Medical Practice Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Ventura (D)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Amends the Medical Practice Act, provides that artificial intelligence has the meaning given to that term in the Human Rights Act, prohibits a person licensed under the Act from allowing artificial intelligence to prescribe medication if the person licensed under the Act does not maintain full control and responsibility for the prescription, establishes penalty provisions for violations of provisions concerning the use of artificial intelligence to prescribe medication.
History: Click for History

IL S 2995
2026
Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Ventura (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, provides that a person shall not use artificial intelligence while engaging in trade and commerce to communicate with a consumer in a manner that may mislead or deceive a reasonable consumer into believing that the consumer is engaging with a human, unless the consumer is notified in a clear and conspicuous manner that the consumer is not engaging with a human.
History: Click for History

IL S 2996
2026
Election Code
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections, Notification
Summary: Amends the Election Code, provides that, if a person, committee, or other entity creates, originally publishes, or originally distributes a qualified political advertisement, the qualified political advertisement shall include, in a clear and conspicuous manner, a statement that the qualified political advertisement was generated in whole or substantially by artificial intelligence, provides for exemptions from the requirement and for civil penalties, injunctive relief, attorney's fees, and costs.
History: Click for History

IL S 3020
2026
Domestic Violence and Harassment
Status: Pending - Senate Third Reading
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Johnson (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Amends the Protective Orders Article of the Code of Criminal Procedure of 1963, changes the definition of harassment, provides that the court may order the respondent to cease harassment, establishes procedures for motions to extend plenary protective orders, provides that the court may order the respondent to stop creating, using, and disseminating.

IL H 3021
2026
Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2025
Author: Rashid (D) Additional Authors: Lilly (D);Mah (D);Buckner (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, provides that it is an unlawful practice for any person to engage in a commercial transaction or trade practice with a consumer in which: the consumer is communicating or otherwise interacting with a chatbot, artificial intelligence agent, avatar, or other computer technology that engages in a textual or Aural conversation.

2026
Fair Patient Billing Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Simmons (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Fair Patient Billing Act, provides that artificial intelligence has the meaning given to that term in the Human Rights Act, prohibits all hospitals from using artificial intelligence to set or influence health care pricing or billing.

IL S 3114
2026
Transparency in Downcoding Act
Status: Pending - Senate Third Reading
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Koehler (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Transparency in Downcoding Act, provides that the Act applies to certain policies of health insurance amended, delivered, issued, or renewed on or after the effective date of the Act, except for employee or employer self-insured health benefit plans under the federal Employee Retirement Income Security Act and health care provided pursuant to the Workers' Compensation Act or the Workers' Occupational Diseases Act.

IL S 3180
2026
Artificial Intelligence Data Privacy Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Ventura (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Data Privacy Act, prohibits a deployer from training an artificial intelligence on a user's data and retaining the training data indefinitely unless specified conditions are satisfied, sets forth provisions concerning a private right of action, provides that a violation of the Act constitutes an unlawful practice under the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act to make a conforming change.
History: Click for History

IL S 3261
2026
Artificial Intelligence Safety
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Public Safety and Child Protection Transparency Act, provides that a frontier artificial intelligence model developer or large chatbot provider shall write, implement, comply with, and clearly and conspicuously publish on its website a public safety and child protection plan, provides that the Attorney General shall establish a mechanism to be used by a large frontier developer, a large chatbot provider, or a member of the public to report a safety incident related.

IL S 3262
2026
Companion Artificial Intelligence Protection Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Audit, Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Companion Artificial Intelligence Protection Act, provides that an operator of a companion artificial intelligence product shall not deploy or operate a companion artificial intelligence product that incorporates specified features, provides for auditing and reporting requirements, provides that an operator shall provide a clear notification during an interaction with a companion artificial intelligence product informing the user that the user is communicating with a companion.

IL S 3263
2026
Artificial Intelligence Provenance Data Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Provenance Data Act, provides that a covered artificial intelligence tool provider shall make available, at no cost to a person, a provenance label reading tool, provides that a covered artificial intelligence tool provider shall include a provenance label in any image, video, or audio content instance created by its artificial intelligence, sets forth requirements for large online platforms, capture device manufacturers, and third-party licensees and hosting.
History: Click for History

IL S 3312
2026
Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Cybersecurity, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, defines frontier model, frontier developer, and large frontier developer, requires large frontier developers to adopt and publish a frontier AI framework addressing catastrophic risk management, transparency, and cybersecurity, mandates reporting of critical safety incidents to the Emergency Management Agency and Office of Homeland Security and establishes civil penalties for noncompliance.

IL S 3364
2026
Human Rights Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Ventura (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Human Rights Act, makes it a civil rights violation under the Act to use artificial intelligence in a real estate transaction without providing notice to the other person engaging in the real estate transaction that the owner, real estate broker or salesman, or agent of one of them is using artificial intelligence to conduct the real estate transaction, makes it a civil rights violation to fail to inform an applicant for a loan or credit card.

IL S 3368
2026
Chatbot Response Liability Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Rezin (R)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Chatbot Response Liability Act, provides that a proprietor of a chatbot that is used as an alternative to a human representative or that provides any substantive response, information, advice, or action may not disclaim liability if the chatbot provides materially misleading, incorrect, contradictory, or harmful information that results in financial loss or other demonstrable harm or that results in bodily harm to the covered user or any third party.
History: Click for History

IL S 3384
2026
Artificial Intelligence Companion Model Safety Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Ellman (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Companion Model Safety Act, provides that an operator shall not operate or provide an artificial intelligence companion to a user unless the artificial intelligence companion contains a protocol to take reasonable efforts to detect and address suicidal ideation or expressions of self-harm by a user to the artificial intelligence companion, provides that an operator shall provide a clear and conspicuous notification to a user that states, either verbally or in text.

IL S 3444
2026
Artificial Intelligence Safety Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Cunningham (D)
Topics: Cybersecurity, Impact Assessment, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Safety Act, provides that a developer of a frontier artificial intelligence model shall not be held liable for critical harms caused by the frontier model if the developer did not intentionally or recklessly cause the critical harms and the developer publishes a safety and security protocol and transparency report on its website, provides that a developer shall be deemed to have complied with these requirements if the developer.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/04/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.
02/17/2026 - To SENATE Committee on EXECUTIVE.
02/18/2026 - In SENATE Committee on EXECUTIVE: Referred to Subcommittee on AI and Social Media.
03/13/2026 - In SENATE. Rule 2-10 Committee Deadline Established As March 27, 2026.
03/27/2026 - In SENATE. Rule 2-10 Committee Deadline Established As April 24, 2026.

IL S 3467
2026
Preventing Sexual Violence in Higher Education Act
Status: Pending - Senate Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Guzman (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Amends the Preventing Sexual Violence in Higher Education Act, makes changes concerning definitions, requires a higher education institution's comprehensive policy to include sexual harassment, provides that a confidential advisor is separate from a complaint advisor, unless a complainant chooses to have the confidential advisor also serve as the complaint advisor, makes changes to the complaint resolution procedure, includes the timeline of the complaint resolution procedure, protective measures.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.
02/17/2026 - To SENATE Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION.
03/11/2026 - From SENATE Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION: Do pass.
03/11/2026 - In SENATE. Placed on Calendar Order Second Reading.

IL S 3492
2026
School Code
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Preston (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Amends the School Code, provides that the State Board of Education may provide statewide guidance to school districts concerning a workforce development curriculum or programming or both that can be adopted at the school district, school, or classroom level and that provides best practices for teaching artificial intelligence, quantum computing, and other types of rapidly growing technologies as deemed necessary.
History: Click for History

02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.

IL S 3502
2026
Artificial Intelligence Design Requirements Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Ventura (D)
Topics: Health Use, Impact Assessment, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Design Requirements Act, sets forth provisions concerning product liability actions brought against a developer of an artificial intelligence system for defective design, failure to contain adequate instructions or warnings, and failure to conform to an express warranty, provides that a deployer of an artificial intelligence system shall be deemed to be liable as a developer for harm caused by a product if the deployer makes material and substantial change.

IL S 3571
2026
Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notifications
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Simmons (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act, provides that, when required to report the reason for a mass layoff or closing, an employer shall disclose artificial intelligence related job impacts, including the number of employees laid off substantially due to the replacement or automation of employee functions by artificial intelligence, provides that, if the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity issues a public report disclosing mass layoffs or closings.

IL S 3590
2026
Artificial Intelligence Product Liability
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Impact Assessment, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Product Liability Act, sets forth provisions concerning product liability actions brought against a developer of an artificial intelligence system for defective design, failure to provide adequate instructions or warnings, and failure to conform to an express warranty, provides that a deployer of an artificial intelligence system shall be deemed liable as a developer for harm caused by a product if the deployer makes a material and substantial change.

IL S 3601
2026
Professional AI Oversight
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Stadelman (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Professional AI Oversight Act, defines terms, provides that a licensee shall prominently disclose when a person who is paying for a service provided by the licensee is interacting with artificial intelligence, provides that the disclosure shall be provided verbally at the start of an oral exchange or conversation and in writing before a written exchange and notify the person of the specific purpose of the artificial intelligence that will be used in the interaction.
History: Click for History

IL S 3657
2026
Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Assignments Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Martwick (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, provides that a grocery store that offers a digital coupon to consumers for the purchase of goods must make available a corresponding physical or paper copy of the coupon of equal value to consumers upon request, provides that the paper coupons shall be easily accessible at the service desk and may also be placed in other locations around the store, provides that a person may not use surveillance-based price setting or use surveillance.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.
03/04/2026 - To SENATE Committee on EXECUTIVE.
03/13/2026 - In SENATE. Rule 2-10 Committee Deadline Established As March 27, 2026.
03/27/2026 - Rule 3-9(a) / Re-referred to ASSIGNMENTS Committee.

IL S 3678
2026
Landlord and Tenant Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Simmons (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Landlord and Tenant Act, prohibits a person from entering into an agreement with any other person to not compete in rental pricing, fees, or any other rental term for residential rental units in the State, prohibits a person from engaging in price coordination for residential rental units in the State.
History: Click for History

IL S 3702
2026
Artificial Intelligence in Nurse Practice Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Guzman (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Responsible Use
Summary: Amends the Nurse Practice Act, relates to provisions concerning registered professional nurses, adds provisions concerning: the use of artificial intelligence in recorded or transcribed encounters, prohibition on substituting artificial intelligence for nursing services, uses of artificial intelligence as clinical decision support under the control of a registered professional nurse, relates to patient notice and transparency, relates to confidentiality protections.

IL S 3735
2026
Student Educational Technologies Rights Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Martwick (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Creates the Student Educational Technologies Rights Act, provides that it is the policy of the state that a student and the student's parent have the right to opt out of school-issued personal electronic devices, electronic textbooks, electronic required reading, or electronic or online assignments, requests a human teacher review any automated scored grade or scored grade generated by artificial intelligence, relates to opts out of predictive analytics systems without academic penalty.
History: Click for History

02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on ASSIGNMENTS.

IL H 3838
2026
Ticket Sale and Resale Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/7/2025
Author: Huynh (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Ticket Sale and Resale Act, provides that a ticket seller, ticket reseller, and ticket broker shall display the full price of a ticket, including all assessed fees, to a purchaser when the price of a ticket is first shown to the purchaser and shall not increase that price during the transaction with the purchaser, provides that the use of dynamic pricing in the course of selling a ticket is a violation of the provision, defines dynamic pricing.

IL S 3901
2026
Marriage Act
Status: Pending - Senate Executive Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2026
Author: Edly-Allen (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Judicial Use
Summary: Amends the Marriage and Dissolution of Marriage Act, provides legislative intent that the misuse of artificial intelligence involves the fabrication of evidence that can be fake, manipulated, or non-existent that can have a profound effect in family law cases, provides that its misuse can be particularly harmful in family law proceedings because of the emotion involved.

IL H 4248
2026
Algorithmic Pricing Transparency Act
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 12/17/2025
Author: Buckner (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Algorithmic Pricing Transparency Act, provides that any person or entity that sells or offers to sell goods or services through an online platform to consumers in this State shall provide a clear and conspicuous disclosure if the price displayed to a consumer is generated using surveillance pricing, provides that a consumer may opt out of surveillance pricing and a covered entity shall provide the consumer with a non-personalized baseline price for the goods or services.
History: Click for History
12/17/2025 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/17/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.

03/24/2026 - In HOUSE. Tabled Amendment No. 1.
03/25/2026 - In HOUSE. Placed on Short Debate Calendar Second Reading.

IL H 4312
2026
Criminal Procedure of 1963
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: West (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Amends the Code of Criminal Procedure of 1963, changes the definitions of harassment to include, among other actions, doxing, electronic tracking, and nonconsensual creation, dissemination, or threatening the dissemination of electronically generated or digitally altered content pertaining to the petitioner, requires a county to provide remote access to a hearing on a protective order to include relevant witnesses besides the petitioner and respondent.
History: Click for History
01/07/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/17/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY - CIVIL.

IL H 4350
2026
Income Tax Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/8/2026
Author: McLaughlin (R)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Taxes
Summary: Amends the Income Tax Act, creates an income tax credit in an amount equal to 100%, but in no event more than $250,000 per taxpayer in any taxable year, of qualified business expenses paid by a qualified small business during the taxable year.
History: Click for History
01/08/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4411
2026
School Code
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Dias (D)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Amends the School Code, requires, beginning with the 2027-2028 school year, school boards to report specified data to the State Board of Education regarding computer science courses in schools maintaining any of grades kindergarten through 8, requires the State Board to make the data publicly available, provides that the State Board shall create an introductory content area endorsement for teaching computer science in grades 5 through 12, with respect to a Professional Educator License.
History: Click for History
01/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4544
2026
Preventing Algorithmic Pricing Discrimination Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/22/2026
Author: Delgado (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Preventing Algorithmic Pricing Discrimination Act, requires any person who knowingly advertises, promotes, labels, or publishes a statement, display, image, offer, or announcement of personalized algorithmic pricing using consumer data specific to a particular individual must disclose that this price was set by an algorithm using the individual's personal data, prohibits the use of algorithmic pricing under certain conditions.
History: Click for History
01/22/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4557
2026
Digital Forgeries in Politics Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Gong-Gershowitz (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Digital Forgeries in Politics Act, provides that an individual depicted in a digital forgery who is an resident and a candidate for office in this State has a cause of action against any person who knowingly distributes, or enters into an agreement with another person to distribute, a digital forgery, relates to the distribution occurs within 90 days before a regular election, relates to the distribution is reasonably likely to harm the reputation or electoral prospects of a candidate.
History: Click for History
01/23/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/17/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY - CIVIL.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4624
2026
Body Camera Footage Disclosure
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Davis (R)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Law Enforcement Officer-Worn Body Camera Act, makes changes concerning the circumstances under which officer-worn body camera footage may be disclosed under the Freedom of Information Act, requires a requester of officer-worn body camera footage under the Freedom of Information Act to certify specified information with the request, exempts officer-worn body camera footage from disclosure if it reveals certain information, depicts certain information, involves an active investigation.
History: Click for History
01/27/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/03/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4674
2026
School Code
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: West (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Amends the School Code, requires a policy on bullying to establish escalating interventions for students who engage in known, pervasive, and persistent bullying, provides that in the event of the death by suicide of a student enrolled in a school district, the school district shall initiate a complete and transparent investigation to determine whether bullying or harassment related to the educational environment played a contributing role, sets forth investigation requirements.
History: Click for History
01/29/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/03/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/17/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ELEMENTARY & SECONDARY EDUCATION: ADMINISTRATION, LICENSING & CHARTER SCHOOLS.

IL H 4705
2026
Artificial Intelligence Public Safety and Child Protect
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Didech (D)
Topics: Audit, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Artificial Intelligence Public Safety and Child Protection Transparency Act, provides that a frontier artificial intelligence model developer or large chatbot provider shall write, implement, comply with, and clearly and conspicuously publish on its website a public safety and child protection plan, provides that the Attorney General shall establish a mechanism to be used by a large frontier developer, a large chatbot provider, or a member of the public to report a safety incident.
History: Click for History
01/29/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY - CIVIL.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4711
2026
Provenance Data Requirements Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Scherer (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Creates the Provenance Data Requirements Act, provides that a generative artificial intelligence tool provider shall apply provenance data, either directly or through the use of third-party technology, to wholly-generated synthetic content generated by the provider's generative artificial intelligence tool, sets forth additional requirements on generative artificial intelligence tool providers, large online platforms, and manufacturers of capture devices, defines terms.
History: Click for History
01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
02/17/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on CYBERSECURITY, DATA ANALYTICS, & IT.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4717
2026
Personalized Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Grant (R)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Personalized Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure Act, provides that any entity that sets the price of goods or services using personalized algorithmic pricing, and that directly or indirectly advertises, promotes, labels, or publishes a statement, display, image, offer, or announcement of personalized algorithmic pricing to a consumer, using personal data specific to the consumer, shall include a specified disclosure, sets forth exceptions to the provision.
History: Click for History
01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4735
2026
Transparency in Downcoding Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Chung (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Transparency in Downcoding Act, provides that the Act applies to certain policies of health insurance amended, delivered, issued, or renewed on or after the effective date of the Act, except for employee or employer self-insured health benefit plans under the federal Employee Retirement Income Security Act and health care provided pursuant to the Workers' Compensation Act or the Workers' Occupational Diseases Act, prohibits a health insurance issuer from using an automated process.
History: Click for History
01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on INSURANCE.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4799
2026
Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: du Buclet (D)
Topics: Cybersecurity, Notification, Oversight/Governance, Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act, requires large frontier artificial intelligence developers to adopt and publish a frontier artificial intelligence framework addressing catastrophic risk management, transparency, and cybersecurity, mandates reporting of critical safety incidents to the Attorney General and establishes civil penalties for noncompliance, directs the Department of Innovation and Technology to review and recommend updates to definitions and standards.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 4804
2026
Nurse Practice Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Morgan (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Amends the Nurse Practice Act, concerns registered professional nurses, adds provisions concerning the use of artificial intelligence in recorded or transcribed encounters, prohibition on substituting artificial intelligence for nursing services, uses of artificial intelligence as clinical decision support under the control of a registered professional nurse, patient notice and transparency, confidentiality protections, exceptions for nonclinical activity, defines terms.
History: Click for History

IL H 4882
2026
Future of Work Act of 2026
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Ugaste (R)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training, Effect on Labor/Employment, Studies
Summary: Creates the Future of Work Act, creates the Future of Work Task Force, describes the duties and responsibilities of the Task Force, provides for the membership and meetings of the Task Force, provides that members of the Task Force shall serve without compensation, provides that the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity shall provide administrative support to the Task Force.

IL H 4945
2026
School Code and Technology Guidance
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Mason (D)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Amends the School Code, provides that the State Board of Education may provide statewide guidance to school districts concerning a workforce development curriculum or programming or both that can be adopted at the school district, school, or classroom level and that provides best practices for teaching artificial intelligence, quantum computing, and other types of rapidly growing technologies as deemed necessary, in a manner that prepares students for workforce, job, and career competencies.

IL H 4980
2026
Artificial Intelligence Act
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Rashid (D)
Topics: Government Use, Impact Assessment, Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Meaningful Human Control of Artificial Intelligence Act, provides that a public employer, including a contractor or subcontractor of the public employer, shall not use or apply, or authorize any procurement, purchase, or acquisition of any service or system using or relying on any automated decision-making system, directly or indirectly, without meaningful and continuing human review when performing specified functions, provides that the definition of ""employee"" includes employee candidates.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on LABOR & COMMERCE.
03/09/2026 - In HOUSE. Committee Amendment No. 1 filed. Referred to RULES.
03/18/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on RULES: Referred to HOUSE Labor & Commerce Committee Amendment No. 1.
03/19/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on LABOR & COMMERCE: Adopted Amendment No. 1.
03/19/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on LABOR & COMMERCE: Do pass as Amended.
03/20/2026 - In HOUSE. Placed on Short Debate Calendar Second Reading.

IL H 4985
2026
Surveillance Based Price Discrimination Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Syed (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Creates the Surveillance-Based Price Discrimination Act, provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized price assessed to a consumer for goods or services, sets forth exemptions to the provision, provides that the Attorney General shall enforce the Act, sets forth provisions concerning private rights of action and penalties for violations.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/18/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4987
2026
Surveillance Based Wage Discrimination Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Syed (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Surveillance-Based Wage Discrimination Act, provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized wage paid to an employee, sets forth exemptions to the provisions, provides that the Attorney General shall enforce the Act, sets forth provisions concerning private rights of action and penalties for violations.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/18/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on LABOR & COMMERCE.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4988
2026
Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Syed (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act, provides that the owner, licensee, or operator of a generative artificial intelligence system shall conspicuously display a warning on the system's user interface notifying users of the limitations of generative artificial intelligence systems, provides that each day the required warning is not displayed by the owner, licensee, or operator of a generative artificial intelligence system constitutes a separate violation of the Act.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/18/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 4990
2026
Preventing Sexual Violence in Higher Education Act
Status: Pending - House Second Reading
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Canty (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Private Right of Action
Summary: Amends the Preventing Sexual Violence in Higher Education Act, makes changes concerning definitions, requires a higher education institution's comprehensive policy to include sexual harassment, provides that a confidential advisor is separate from a complaint advisor, unless a complainant chooses to have the confidential advisor also serve as the complaint advisor, makes changes to the complaint resolution procedure, includes the timeline of the complaint resolution procedure.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION.
03/25/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION: Do pass.
03/26/2026 - In HOUSE. Placed on Short Debate Calendar Second Reading.

IL H 5003
2026
Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Morgan (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Amends the Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act, defines qualified research program, provides that the prohibitions on unauthorized therapy services under the Act shall not apply to artificial intelligence-assisted therapy or psychotherapy services provided exclusively within a qualified research program, sets forth requirements for qualified research programs, requires a licensed professional participating in a qualified research program to perform certain actions.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on HEALTH CARE LICENSES.
03/16/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on HEALTH CARE LICENSES: To Healthcare AI Subcommittee.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 5044
2026
Chatbot Provider Liability Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Gong-Gershowitz (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Creates the Chatbot Provider Liability Act, provides that for purposes of the Act, chatbots are deemed products for the purpose of strict liability as other product liability actions, provides that a chatbot provider has a duty to ensure that the use of its chatbot does not cause injury to a user and is liable for any injury it caused a user through the use of its chatbot regardless of whether the chatbot provider exercised all reasonable care in the design and distribution of the chatbot.
History: Click for History
02/04/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY - CIVIL.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 5106
2026
deepfake
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: West (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Amends the Criminal Code of 2012, provides that a person also commits harassment through electronic communications when he or she uses electronic communication to transmit to any person a deepfake with intent to harass, defines deepfake.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

IL H 5113
2026
School Code
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Syed (D)
Topics:
Summary: Amends the School Code, requires the State Board of Education to establish an Artificial-Intelligence Use in Education Commission to collect information and submit recommendations relative to best educational practices and policies for smartphone and artificial-intelligence use in schools and compile research and collaborate to learn more on what recommendations are Most effective for student-learning outcomes.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/18/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on EDUCATION POLICY.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

IL H 5323
2026
Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Rashid (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends the Personalized Algorithmic Pricing Disclosure Act, provides that any entity that sets the price of goods or services using personalized algorithmic pricing, and that directly or indirectly advertises, promotes, labels, or publishes a statement, display, image, offer, or announcement of personalized algorithmic pricing to a consumer, using personal data specific to the consumer, shall include a specified disclosure, sets forth exceptions to the provision.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.
03/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.
03/27/2026 - Rule 19(a)/ Re-referred to RULES Committee.

2026
Department of Innovation and Technology
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/18/2026
Author: Gabel (D)
Topics: Appropriations
Summary: Makes appropriations for the ordinary and contingent expenses of the Department of Innovation and Technology for the specified fiscal year.
History: Click for History
02/18/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/19/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on RULES.

Indiana
IN S 173
2026
Health Care Anesthesia Coverage Protections
Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/15/2026
Author: Johnson (R)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relates to health care matters, prohibits, the state employee health plan, the Medicaid program, an accident and sickness insurance policy, and a health maintenance organization individual or group contract, from imposing a time limit on the amount of anesthesia time for a medical procedure or otherwise restricting or excluding coverage or payment of anesthesia time, modifies the definitions of charity care and community benefits for purposes of certain hospital reporting requirements.
History: Click for History
01/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on HEALTH AND PROVIDER SERVICES.
01/15/2026 - From SENATE Committee on HEALTH AND PROVIDER SERVICES: Do pass as amended.
01/15/2026 - Committee amendment adopted on SENATE floor.
01/15/2026 - Reassigned to SENATE Committee on APPROPRIATIONS.

IN H 1099
2026
Foreign Adversaries
Status: Failed - Adjourned - Senate Homeland Security & Transportation Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/29/2026
Author: Commons (R)
Topics: Cybersecurity, Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that, if a contractor or a subcontractor working under the contractor for the provision of a technological product or service is a prohibited person, a qualified entity may enter into the contract if the contractor affirms the contractor and its subcontractors are not acting as agents of a foreign adversary, requires a state educational institution to meet certain requirements regarding the admission and enrollment of a foreign student into qualifying programs at state educational institutions.
History: Click for History
12/30/2025 - INTRODUCED.
01/05/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY.
01/27/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY: Do pass as amended.
01/27/2026 - Committee amendment adopted on HOUSE floor.
01/29/2026 - Amended on HOUSE floor.
01/29/2026 - Ordered Engrossed.
02/02/2026 - Passed HOUSE. *****To SENATE.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on HOMELAND SECURITY AND TRANSPORTATION.

IN H 1182
2026
Digital Sexual Image Abuse
Status: Failed - Adjourned - House Courts and Criminal Code Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Bauer M (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Relates to digital sexual image abuse, defines digital sexual image as a digital, computer generated image or video that is created or modified to depict, sexual intercourse, other sexual conduct, or exhibition of certain body parts, of a quality, characteristic, or condition, such that it appears to depict an individual, establishes the offenses of possession of a digital sexual image and distribution of a digital sexual image.
History: Click for History
01/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/05/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COURTS AND CRIMINAL CODE.

IN H 1183
2026
Digital Sexual Image Abuse
Status: Failed - Adjourned - House Courts and Criminal Code Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Haggard (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Relates to digital sexual image abuse, increases the penalty for the crime of distribution of an intimate image, from a Class A misdemeanor to a Level 6 felony, and increases the enhancement of this crime from a Level 6 felony to a Level 5 felony, defines digital sexual image as a digital, computer generated image or video that is created or modified to depict, sexual intercourse, other sexual conduct, or exhibition of certain body parts, of a quality, characteristic, or condition.
History: Click for History
01/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/05/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COURTS AND CRIMINAL CODE.

IN H 1201
2026
Mental Health Insurance Compliance
Status: Failed - Adjourned - House Insurance Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/5/2026
Author: Rowray (R)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relates to various mental health and insurance matters, prohibits the use of an artificial intelligence system to impersonate or act as a substitute for a licensed mental health professional, requires the department of insurance to contract with an objective third party to verify that health carriers are in compliance with network adequacy standards, sets forth notice requirements for an amendment to a health provider contract, prohibits the use of downcoding in a specified manner.
History: Click for History
01/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/05/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on INSURANCE.

IN H 1271
2026
Health Claims Payment
Status: Enacted - Act No. 88-2026
Date of Last Action: 03/04/2026 - Enacted
Author: McGuire (R)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Requires a hospital to disclose information concerning payment assistance programs, post signs concerning the programs in specified locations of the hospital, and make information concerning the programs available to individuals through the hospital's patient portal, requires a hospital to make a reasonable effort to notify individuals of available payment assistance programs before beginning a collection action against the individual, prohibits the use of downcoding in a specified manner.

IN H 1303
2026
Sex Crimes and Child Safety
Status: Enacted - Act No. 92-2026
Date of Last Action: 03/04/2026 - Enacted
Author: McNamara (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Specifies the sex offender reporting requirements for persons who are sex offenders or required to register in other jurisdictions, adds high risk missing person to the definition of missing child, specifies that the silver alert program applies to missing children, removes provisions concerning possession of child sex abuse material (CSAM) from the statute containing the offense of child exploitation, and makes a new section for CSAM offenses.

IN H 1421
2026
Employer Bans Automated Decision System Use
Status: Failed - Adjourned - House Employment, Labor and Pensions Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/8/2026
Author: Harris (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to ban on employer use of automated decision systems, prohibits an employer from, relying exclusively on an automated decision system in making an employment related decision with respect to a covered individual, using an automated decision system output in making an employment related decision with respect to a covered individual unless certain conditions are met, and discriminating or retaliating against a covered individual for exercising rights under these provisions.
History: Click for History
01/08/2026 - INTRODUCED.

Kansas
KS S 405
2026
Train Artificial Intelligence for Unlawful Actions
Status: Pending - Senate Federal and State Affairs Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Federal and State Affairs Cmt
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Makes it unlawful for a person to knowingly train artificial intelligence to encourage or support suicide or the unlawful killing of another person, provide emotional support, develop emotional relationships, act as a healthcare professional, simulate humans or encourage isolation.
History: Click for History
01/28/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To SENATE Committee on FEDERAL AND STATE AFFAIRS.
02/04/2026 - Senate Hearing: Monday, February 9, 2026, 10:30 AM Room 144-S.

KS S 449
2026
Clean Air Preservation Act
Status: Pending - Senate Federal and State Affairs Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Federal and State Affairs Cmt
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Enacts the clean air preservation act to prohibit solar radiation modification, geoengineering, weather modification, cloud seeding and other polluting atmospheric experiments or interventions and creating a crime for violation thereof.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/03/2026 - To SENATE Committee on FEDERAL AND STATE AFFAIRS.

KS S 467
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence in Medical Decisions
Status: Pending - Senate Financial Institutions and Insurance Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Holscher (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Oversight/Governance
Summary: Enacts the use of artificial intelligence in medical decisions transparency act and requiring that all medical necessity determinations be made by a competent licensed physician or healthcare professional.
History: Click for History
02/03/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/04/2026 - To SENATE Committee on FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS and INSURANCE.

KS S 493
2026
Crime of Sexual Exploitation of a Child
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Judiciary Cmt
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Adds an element to the crime of sexual exploitation of a child that involves communication through electronic means and social media and providing criminal penalties therefor.
History: Click for History
02/05/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/06/2026 - To SENATE Committee on JUDICIARY.

KS S 499
2026
Risks of Compulsive Use of Digital Products in Minors
Status: Pending - Senate Federal and State Affairs Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/9/2026
Author: Federal and State Affairs Cmt
Topics: Deepfake, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Enacts the State age-appropriate design code act to require businesses to assess and mitigate risks of compulsive use in minors, enacts the State stopping likeness abuse by nonconsensual digital replicas act to create a private right of action for the unauthorized digital replication and distribution of individuals' digital likenesses, enacts the State saving human connection act to prohibit deceptive practices and ensure transparency in chatbot interactions.
History: Click for History
02/09/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To SENATE Committee on FEDERAL AND STATE AFFAIRS.

KS H 2183
2026
State Agencies
Status: Enacted - Act No. 1
Date of Last Action: 02/06/2026 - Enacted
Author: Judiciary Cmt
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Concerns state agencies, relates to interpretation of statutes, rules and regulations and documents with the force and effect of law, prohibits deference to the agency's interpretation by a state court or an administrative hearing officer hearing an administrative action.

KS H 2518
2026
Crime of Breach of Privacy
Status: To Governor - Eligible for Governor
Date of Last Action: 3/27/2026
Author: Judiciary Cmt
Topics: Child Pornography, Deepfake
Summary: Concerns crimes, punishment and criminal procedure, relates to breach of privacy, increases penalties for attempt, conspiracy or criminal solicitation to commit certain violations of such crime, modifies the culpable mental state required to commit such crime, increases penalties for certain violations if the victim is less than 18 years of age.

KS H 2537
2026
Sexual Extortion
Status: To Governor - To Governor
Date of Last Action: 2/17/2026
Author: Judiciary Cmt
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Concerns crimes, punishment and criminal procedure, relates to sexual extortion, requires the attorney general to prepare and provide educational materials and information concerning such crime, increases the penalties for such crime when an offender is 18 years of age or older and the victim is less than 18 years of age or a dependent adult, creates the crimes of aggravated sexual extortion causing great bodily harm and aggravated sexual extortion causing death.
History: Click for History

KS H 2594
2026
Elements of the Crime of Blackmail
Status: Pending - Conference Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Judiciary Cmt
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Relates to blackmail, modifies the elements related to threatened dissemination of any image, video or other recording of another identifiable person who is nude or engaged in sexual activity by changing the connection to breach of privacy and describing the content of the image, video or other recording, provides that blackmail is intentionally gaining or attempting to gain anything of value or compelling or attempting to compel another to act against such persons will, with certain exclusions.

KS H 2671
2026
State Community Harmed by AI Technology Act
Status: Pending - House Legislative Modernization Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: K-12 Education Budget Cmt
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Establishes the Kansas community harmed by AI technology act, mandating user accounts and age verification for AI chatbot access, classifying users by age, requiring parental consent for minors, blocking explicit content, protecting age information confidentiality, monitoring for suicidal ideation, informing users of AI interaction, requiring compliance guidance by specified date, outlining enforcement under consumer protection laws and providing safe harbor for compliant entities.
History: Click for History
02/03/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/04/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on LEGISLATIVE MODERNIZATION.

Summary: Enacts the Kansas Age-appropriate Design Code Act to require businesses to assess and mitigate risks of compulsive use in minors, enacts the Kansas stopping likeness abuse by nonconsensual digital replicas act to create a private right of action for the unauthorized digital replication and distribution of individuals' digital likenesses, enacts the Kansas saving human connection act to prohibit deceptive practices and ensure transparency in chatbot interactions.
History: Click for History
02/10/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/11/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on FEDERAL AND STATE AFFAIRS.

KS HR 6023
2026
Regulate Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - House Legislative Modernization Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Legislative Modernization Cmt
Topics: Deepfake, Elections, Judicial Use, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Opposes the federal preemption of state laws that regulate artificial intelligence.
History: Click for History
01/28/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/29/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on LEGISLATIVE MODERNIZATION.

Kentucky
KY H 33
2026
Data Privacy
Status: Pending - House Small Business and Information Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/6/2026
Author: Moore A (D) Additional Authors: Brown (D);Gentry (D);Banta (R);Sharp (R);Aull (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Defines automated-decision system, base price, individualized data, and surveillance pricing, prohibits any controller under the Kentucky Consumer Data Protection Act from engaging in surveillance pricing, or offering, setting, or displaying a price for a good or service to a consumer using an automated-decision system that is based, in whole or in part, on individualized data, provides that the Act may be cited as the Kentucky Price Fairness Act.
History: Click for History
01/06/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/06/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMITTEES.
01/13/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on SMALL BUSINESS & INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY.

KY H 63
2026
Privacy Protection
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/7/2026
Author: Hodgson (R) Additional Authors: Johnson (R);Baker (R);Lockett (R);Bratcher S (R);Callaway (R);Grossberg (D);Jackson (R);Proctor (R);Bivens (R);Lewis C (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Private Right of Action
Summary: Defines terms, establishes limitations on disseminating deep fakes, and specify exceptions, establishes a statute of limitations for an action filed for the unlawful dissemination of a deep fake, establishes a criminal penalty for illegally disseminating a deep fake.
History: Click for History
01/07/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/07/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMITTEES.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY.

KY S 175
2026
Health Command
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Committees Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Meredith (R)
Topics: Health Use, Oversight/Governance
Summary: Defines terms, establishes the State Health Command in the Commonwealth Office of Technology, requires the State Health Command to contract with a private sector entity to develop and provide standards and qualifications for the utilization of an artificial intelligence-assisted virtual health platform, establishes a process by which rural health care providers and facilities may contract for use of the artificial intelligence-assisted virtual health platform, establishes a process for distribution.
History: Click for History
02/10/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/10/2026 - To SENATE Committee on COMMITTEES.

KY H 201
2026
Use of Algorithmic Devices
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/7/2026
Author: Kulkarnir (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Defines algorithmic device, prohibits the use of algorithmic devices in setting the amount of rent to be charged to a residential tenant and provide that such use violates KRS 367.170 and 367.175 of the Consumer Protection Act, provides that the prohibition shall apply to a landlord's calculation of rent for a rental agreement executed on or after the effective date of the Act.
History: Click for History
01/07/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/07/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMITTEES.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY.

KY H 227
2026
Addictive Online Platforms
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/9/2026
Author: Lockett (R) Additional Authors: Decker (R);Roberts T (R);Lehman (D);Grossl (R);Proctor (R);Massaroni (R);Hodgson (R);Grossberg (D);Doan (R);Chester-Burton (D);Callaway (R);Rabourn (R);Imes (R);Flannery (R);Dotson (R);Rudy (R);Calloway (R);Baker (R);Banta (R);Tate (R);Huff T (R);Gordon (R);Bridges (R);Thomas (R);Nemes (R);Moser (R);Fugate (R);Fleming (R);Brown (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Establishes requirements to protect minors from AI companion platforms and social media platforms using addictive features and predatory data collection, defines terms, requires AI companion platforms and social media platforms to refine their age verification for users, prohibits AI companion platforms or social media platforms from maintaining accounts for children without verifiable parental consent, creates a private right of action for violations by AI companion platforms or social media platforms.
History: Click for History

KY H 318
2026
Property Rights
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/12/2026
Author: Grossberg (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Defines terms, establishes property rights in every individual's name, voice, and likeness, establishes how the property rights may be transferred or terminated, establishes a civil cause of action and damages for unauthorized use of an individual's name, voice, or likeness, provides exceptions, requires that an action commence within 4 years of discovery of the violation.
History: Click for History

01/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMITTEES.

KY H 366
2026
Sexual Performance by a Minor
Status: To Governor - To Governor
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Johnson (R) Additional Authors: Moser (R);Grossberg (D)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Provides that person is guilty of possession or viewing of matter portraying a sexual performance by a minor when, having knowledge of its content, character, and that the sexual performance is by a minor or computer-generated image of a minor, he or she, among others, knowingly has in his or her possession or control any matter which visually depicts a sexual performance by a minor person or computer-generated image of a minor person.

KY H 455
2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - Senate Committee on Committees Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/23/2026
Author: Banta (R) Additional Authors: Willner (D);Chester-Burton (D);Stalker (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Defines terms, restricts the use of artificial intelligence by certain licensed professionals in therapy and psychotherapy services, prohibits licensed professionals from using artificial intelligence to assist in providing supplementary support in therapy or psychotherapy services where the client's therapeutic session is recorded, provides exceptions, prohibits advertising or offering therapy or psychotherapy services unless the therapy or psychotherapy services are conducted by a licensed.

KY H 559
2026
Data Privacy and Interoperability
Status: Pending - House Small Business and Information Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/4/2026
Author: Grossl (R)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Establishes consumer rights relating to social media and artificial intelligence data, defines terms, requires social media companies and model operators to implement data interoperability interfaces, establishes requirements for data sharing between social media services and artificial intelligence systems, grants rulemaking authority to the Attorney General, along with the Consumers' Advisory Council, and enforcement authority to the Office of the Attorney General, provides for civil penalties.

KY H 641
2026
Mental Health Chatbots
Status: Pending - House Small Business and Information Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Grossl (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Protects consumers using mental health chatbots, defines terms, establishes prohibitions and exceptions for how suppliers are to handle individually identifiable health information, prohibits supplier from advertising a specific product or service unless that product or service is clearly and conspicuously disclosed, requires supplier to clearly and conspicuously disclose that the mental health chatbot is artificial intelligence and provide when this disclosure should appear.

2026

Status: To Governor - To Governor
Date of Last Action: 4/1/2026
Author: Petrie (R) Additional Authors: Burke (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections
Summary: Presumes that a service provided to a purchaser in this state, was purchased from a retailer for storage, use, or other consumption in this state, requires the Department for Local Government to report to the Legislative Research Commission for referral to the Interim Joint Committee on Appropriations and Revenue the total amount of funds distributed to each agency for the fiscal year, how each agency expended the funds, and the results of the use of funds in terms of economic development and job creation.

ME H 343
2026
Technology Facilitated Sexual Abuse
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Kuhn (D) Additional Authors: Daughtry (D);Cloutier (D);Arford (D);Roeder (D);Sachs (D);Sargent (D);Sinclair (D);Beck (D);O'Halloran (D)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Specifies when a person is guilty of sexual exploitation of a minor, provides that a person is guilty of dissemination of child sexual abuse material if that person, among other things, intentionally or knowingly disseminates or possesses with intent to disseminate any obscene material that is child sexual abuse material, including an image created using generative AI or machine learning, and the person knows or has reason to know that the material is child sexual abuse material, classifies such crimes.

Maryland
MD S 8
2026
Personal Identifying Information Prohibition
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/18/2026
Author: Hester (D) Additional Authors: Benson (D);James (D);Waldstreicher (D);Muse (D);Folden (R);Hettleman (D);Smith W (D);Sydnor (D);West (R);Charles (D);Love (D);McKay (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Associated Bills: MD H 184 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Prohibits a person from utilizing certain personal identifying information or engaging in certain conduct in order to cause physical injury, serious emotional distress, or economic damages, prohibits a person from using certain artificial intelligence or certain deepfake representations for certain purposes, provides penalties of specified years of imprisonment and a fine of up specified amount or both where one victim is involved.

Topics: Government Use
Associated Bills: MD S 114 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Establishes the Maryland 3-1-1 Oversight Board in the Maryland Information Network to oversee the expansion of 3-1-1 systems in the State, establishes the 3-1-1 Program to use certain artificial intelligence to answer certain questions and route certain calls in certain counties, requires the expansion of the 3-1-1 Program to all of the counties in the State after a certain time period.

MD S 141
2026
Election Misinformation
Status: Pending - House Government, Labor, and Elections Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Hester (D) Additional Authors: Kagan (D);Feldman (D);Washington (D);Brooks (D);Harris (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections
Associated Bills: MD H 145 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Requires the State Administrator of Elections to take certain actions if the State Administrator receives a credible report that election misinformation or election disinformation, including a deepfake, has been or is being communicated, disseminated, or distributed, requires the Administrator to communicate correct information to the public, prohibits a person, under certain circumstances, from knowingly or with reckless disregard using or disseminating a deepfake to produce materially false information.

Summary: Prohibits a person from possessing more than specified number of images of certain child pornography or possessing or viewing images of certain child pornography depicting a minor or an individual indistinguishable from an actual and identifiable child under the specified age, provides that a violation of the Act is a felony and on conviction a violator is subject to imprisonment not exceeding specified years.

MD H 314
2026
Automation Technology Deployment Assessment
Status: Failed - Withdrawn from further consideration
Date of Last Action: 1/15/2026
Author: Stewart (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires employers with specified number of employees that reduced their workforce in the State by a specified number of employees while using automation technology to submit certain information to the Secretary of Labor concerning the number of employees employed, automation technology deployed or used, and certain employees separated from employment during the immediately preceding calendar year, requires a certain covered employer to pay a certain assessment for each displaced employee.

MD S 375
2026
Artificial Intelligence Training Program
Status: Pending - Senate Education, Energy, and the Environment Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Watson R (D)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Requires the State Department of Education to develop or approve an artificial intelligence training program for members of county boards of education that includes foundational training and continuing annual training, requires each member of a county board to complete an artificial intelligence training program each year that covers developments in artificial intelligence technology, evolving best practices for use in education settings and new guidance, provides the training may be in person or online.

MD S 387
2026

Status: Pending - House Economic Matters Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/20/2026
Author: Ferguson (D) Additional Authors: Kagan (D);King N (D);Zucker (D);Brooks (D);Hettleman (D);Lam (D);Sydnor (D);Charles (D);Love (D);Augustine (D);Hester (D);Harris (D);Lewis Young (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: MD H 895 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Prohibits a food retailer from engaging in the practice of dynamic pricing or using consumer surveillance data to set a price for consumer goods or services, prohibits a food retailer from using protected class data to offer, advertise, or sell a consumer good or service under certain circumstances, prohibits a food retailer from diminishing or impairing any right or benefit guaranteed to employees of the food retailer under an existing collective bargaining agreement or memorandum of understanding.

Status: Pending - House Government, Labor, and Elections Committee

Author: Feldman (D) Additional Authors: Hester (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections
