02/12/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/05 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1153
2026
Claims for Reimbursement
Status: Pending - House Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/11/2026
Author: Guzzone (D) Additional Authors: Kaiser (D);Hill (D);Lehman (D);Qi (D);Watson (D);Jones (D);Pruski (D);Taveras (D);Woods (D);Moreno (D)
Topics: Health Use
Associated Bills: MD S 797 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Prohibits insurers, nonprofit health service plans, health maintenance organizations, and managed care organization downcodes a claim for reimbursement under certain circumstances, establishes certain procedures that insurers, nonprofit health service plans, health maintenance organizations, and managed care organizations are required to follow if the insurer, nonprofit health service plan, or health maintenance organization intends or makes a final decision to downcode a claim.
History: Click for History
02/11/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/11/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on HEALTH.
02/13/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/05 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1250
2026
Consumer Protection and Product Liability Chatbots
Status: Pending - House Economic Matters Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/12/2026
Author: Qi (D) Additional Authors: Rose (R);Queen (D);Fair (D);Miller (R);Tomlinson (R);Coley (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Associated Bills: MD S 827 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Regulates the manner in which a developer designs and creates and an operator makes available to users in the state a chatbot, including establishing safety and privacy protections for users, establishes, an enhanced protection for users who are minors under the age of 13 years, requires the display of certain warnings when using a chatbot, establishes that a chatbot is considered a product for certain product liability actions, applies certain provisions of the Act to governmental units.
History: Click for History
02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC MATTERS.
02/17/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/10 at 1:00 p.m.
02/18/2026 - Hearing cancelled.
02/18/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/03 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1261
2026
Artificial Intelligence Toy Safety Act
Status: Pending - House Economic Matters Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/12/2026
Author: Coley (D) Additional Authors: Wilkins (D);Rogers (D);Amprey (D);Pasteur (D);Tomlinson (R);Young C (D);Mireku-North (D);Wims (D);Ruff (D);Roberts (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Establishes certain child safety and data privacy requirements for certain toys features artificial intelligence, requires an artificial intelligence toy to contain certain label, prohibits the manufacturer of an artificial intelligence toy from using certain data for certain marketing.
History: Click for History
02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC MATTERS.
02/17/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/10 at 1:00 p.m.
02/18/2026 - Hearing cancelled.
02/18/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/03 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1294
2026
Child Sexual Abuse Material
Status: Pending - Senate Judicial Proceedings Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/12/2026
Author: Wilson (D)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Alters the term child pornography to be child sexual abuse material for purposes of certain criminal offenses, defines computer-generated image to include images created through the use of artificial intelligence software as the term pertains to provisions of law related to child sexual abuse material.
History: Click for History
02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/12/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY.
02/16/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/05 at 1:00 p.m.
03/13/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY: Reported favorably.
03/13/2026 - Committee Report adopted on HOUSE floor.
03/13/2026 - Second reading passed. To third reading.
03/18/2026 - Passed HOUSE. *****To SENATE.
03/19/2026 - To SENATE Committee on JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS.
03/20/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/26 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1385
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - House Health Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Hill (D) Additional Authors: Kaiser (D);Turner (D);Palakovic Carr (D);Ruth (D);White Holland (D);Wu (D);Schindler (D);Moreno (D)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Requires that certain audits and compliance reviews of an artificial intelligence, algorithm, or other software tool used for utilization review include a certain evaluation by a licensed health care professional, requires that the review and revision of the performance, use, and outcomes of an artificial intelligence, algorithm, or other software tool used for utilization review include a certain human evaluation and use of the findings of the evaluation for a certain purpose.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/13/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on HEALTH.
02/13/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/12 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1399
2026
Consumer Reporting Agencies
Status: Pending - House Economic Matters Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Hill (D)
Topics: Government Use, Notification, Provenance
Summary: Establishes requirements for consumer reporting agencies that use algorithmic systems to assemble or evaluate consumer credit information on consumers for the purpose of furnishing consumer reports to third parties, requires the Commissioner of Financial Regulation of the Maryland Department of Labor to establish certain assessment thresholds for algorithms, mandate regular training for human reviewers, and implement a certain whistleblower protection program.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/13/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC MATTERS.
02/17/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/10 at 1:00 p.m.

MD H 1473
2026
Economic Development
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Jones A (D)
Topics: Impact Assessment
Associated Bills: MD S 770 - Crossfiled with
Summary: Establishes the Maryland's Future Board under the Department of Commerce to develop, evaluate, and revise a visionary plan for the future of Maryland by a certain date and make recommendations for projects based on the plan to the Governor and the General Assembly, establishes the Maryland's Future Fund as a special, nonlapsing fund, requires interest earnings of the Fund to be credited to the Fund.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/13/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC MATTERS.
02/16/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/03 at 1:00 p.m.

03/17/2026 - Committee Report adopted on HOUSE floor.

03/18/2026 - Passed HOUSE. *****To SENATE.
03/19/2026 - To SENATE Committee on FINANCE.
04/06/2026 - From SENATE Committee on FINANCE: Reported as amended.

MD H 1475
2026
Dynamic Pricing Disclosure and Prohibition on Rent
Status: Pending - House Economic Matters Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/13/2026
Author: Jones A (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits a merchant from setting the price of consumer goods or services using personalized algorithmic pricing and issuing a certain communication in a certain manner unless the merchant includes a certain disclosure, prohibits a person from knowingly or recklessly operating or licensing in a certain manner to perform a coordinating function to facilitate a certain agreement, prohibits residential rental property owners or managers from knowingly or recklessly setting or adjusting rental terms.
History: Click for History
02/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/13/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on ECONOMIC MATTERS.
02/16/2026 - Hearing Scheduled 3/03 at 1:00 p.m.

Minnesota
MN H 1606
2026
Access to Nudification Technology
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Finance and Civil Law Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/16/2026
Author: Hanson (DFL) Additional Authors: Davids (R);Bahner (DFL);Moller (DFL);Stephenson (DFL);Feist (DFL);Frazier (DFL);Smith (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Associated Bills: MN S 1119 - Companion
Summary: Relates to commerce, prohibits access to nudification technology, provides that a person who owns or controls a website, application, software, program, or other service must not allow a user to access, download, or use the website, application, software, program, or other service to nudify an image or video, or nudify an image or video on behalf of a user, provides that no person may advertise or promote any website, application, software, program, or other service that performs the actions described.
History: Click for History
02/26/2025 - INTRODUCED.
02/26/2025 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMERCE FINANCE AND POLICY.
03/16/2026 - From HOUSE Committee on COMMERCE FINANCE AND POLICY: Do pass as amended.
03/16/2026 - Rereferred to HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY FINANCE AND CIVIL LAW.

Mississippi
MS H 544
2026
Institutions of Higher Learning Grant Program
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/12/2026
Author: Zuber (R)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Provides that the state Development Authority shall establish a program to provide grants to state institutions of higher learning to assist such institutions with establishing endowments for the purpose of recruiting and employing professors of quantum computing and professors of artificial intelligence engineering, provides an application process for state institutions of higher learning that desire to participate in the grant program, creates the state institutions of higher learning quantum computing.

MS H 840
2026
Criminal Offenses Enhanced Penalties
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: Ford (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Imposes an enhanced penalty on defendants who knowingly and intentionally used an artificial intelligence system in the commission of a designated offense, provides if the designated offense is a misdemeanor, the defendant shall be subject to an additional term of imprisonment for a period of not less than six nor more than twelve months and a fine not to exceed five thousand dollars, provides that if the designated offense is a felony, the defendant shall be subject to an additional term of imprisonment.
History: Click for History
01/16/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/16/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY B.
02/03/2026 - Died in committee.

MS H 1048
2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: Ford (R)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of artificial intelligence for providing professional mental and behavioral health care, prohibits an artificial intelligence provider from making any representation or statement that an artificial intelligence system is capable of providing such care or programming a system to provide such care, prohibits a person licensed to provide professional mental and behavioral health care using artificial intelligence in his or her practice, provides an exception for licensees.
History: Click for History
01/16/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/16/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on PUBLIC HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES.
01/16/2026 - Additionally referred to HOUSE Committee on ACCOUNTABILITY, EFFICIENCY, TRANSPARENCY.
02/03/2026 - Died in committee.

MS H 1115
2026
Private Image
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Ford (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Includes material that has been edited, altered or otherwise manipulated from its original form in the definition of visual material for the criminal offense of disclosing intimate visual materials, prohibits a person from creating, recording or altering visual material depicting another individual with the depicted individual's intimate parts exposed or engaged in sexual conduct without the consent of the depicted individual, exempts a developer or provider of technology from liability.
History: Click for History
01/19/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/19/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY B.
02/03/2026 - Died in committee.

2026
Dyslexia Generative Artificial Intelligence Education
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Roberson (R)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Establishes the State Dyslexia Generative Artificial Intelligence Education and Workforce Development Act, creates a State funded, open access dyslexia curriculum and generative artificial intelligence system, designates Lighthouse Academy for Dyslexia and the State Dyslexia Institute as founding development partners, provides for State approved curriculum status, authorizes development, compensation, research, and implementation support, provides for pilot programs in schools and correctional facilities.

MS H 1504
2026
Right To Name Likeness and Voice Act
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Clark B (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Establishes a short title for the Stateans' Right to Name, Likeness and Voice Act, defines terms, provides that every individual has a property right in their own name, likeness and voice, provides certain liability for persons or entities who infringe on an individual's rights under this act, provides a first amendment defense to civil actions brought under this act, provides liability for the commercial use of an individual's name, voice or likeness, States the relationship of the act to other laws.

01/19/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on JUDICIARY A.

2026
Artificial General Intelligence Sexual Assault
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Zuber (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits sexual assault against artificial general intelligence (AGI), establishes penalties for such offenses, prohibits the knowing commercial distribution or advertising of AGI intimacy compatible systems for sexual exploitation without required safeguards, provides civil remedies against commercial entities that fail to perform safeguards as required by this act, provides for the purpose of possible amendment, makes minor grammatical corrections.

MS H 1717
2026
Medical Judgement Protection Act
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Powell (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Creates the State Medical Judgement Protection Act, states legislative findings, defines terms, requires providers or facilities using artificial intelligence that materially contributes to a diagnosis, treatment plan, problem list entry, order or clinical note to disclose such use in the medical record, requires a licensed clinician to review and approve any artificial intelligence output used for such purposes, provides certain notice requirements, requires the maintenance of tamper resistant audit logs.

2026

Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/19/2026
Author: Yancey (R)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of artificial intelligence for providing professional mental and behavioral health care, prohibits a person licensed to provide professional mental and behavioral health care using artificial intelligence in his or her practice, provides an exception for licensees using artificial intelligence for certain administrative and supplementary support services, provides that a licensee who violates this act is subject to disciplinary action by the licensing board.

2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Enacted - Signed by Governor
Date of Last Action: 03/09/2026 - Enacted

2026
Artificial Intelligence Used Political Advertisements
Status: Failed - Died

Author: Blackmon B (D)
Topics: Elections

2026
Images or Audio Altered or Created By Digitization
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/8/2026
Author: Johnson (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake
Summary: Criminalizes the unlawful dissemination or publication of an intimate or nonintimate image or audio created or altered by digitization where the image or audio is disseminated or published with intent to cause harm to the emotional, financial or physical welfare of another person and the actor knew or reasonably should have known that the person depicted did not consent to such dissemination or publication, defines terms, provides that the crimes include the use of images or audio created or altered.

2026
Future Innovators Act
Status: To Governor - Eligible for Governor
Date of Last Action: 4/1/2026
Author: Education Cmt
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Creates new specified sections State Code of 1972, to provide that, beginning with the entering ninth-grade class of 2029-2030, a public high school student shall, before graduation, be required to earn one unit of credit in a high-school computer science course, or one unit of credit in an industry-aligned career and technical education with embedded computer science course, provides the state graduation requirements that may be satisfied by either of these courses.

New Hampshire
NH S 640
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - House Executive Departments and Administration Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/7/2026
Author: Office of Pearl
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of artificial intelligence or other means to provide services requiring a professional license unless those services are provided by someone with such a license, allows the office of professional licensure and certification to collect fees for the costs of enforcement of the prohibition on unlicensed practice, establishes specific requirements for the use of artificial intelligence for psychologists, mental health practitioners, and alcohol and other drug use professionals.

2026

NJ S 451
2026
Algorithmic Systems to Influence Price
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 3/5/2026
Author: Stack (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: NJ A 3326 - Identical
Summary: Provides that it shall be unlawful and a violation of the State Antitrust Act for a rental property owner to subscribe to, contract with, or otherwise exchange any form of consideration in return for the use of services of a coordinator, defined as any person who operates a software or data analytics service that performs a coordinating function for any rental property owner, provides that a coordinator shall not include a government entity that sets or limits rents or sale prices of residential property.

NJ S 680
2026
Artificial Intelligence Data Centers Energy Usage Plans
Status: Pending - Senate Budget and Appropriations Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Smith B (D)
Topics: Energy
Associated Bills: NJ A 1170 - Identical
Summary: Requires energy usage plans for proposed artificial intelligence data centers and cryptocurrency mining facilities, requires electricity for such centers and facilities to be derived from new clean energy sources, provides that an energy usage plan shall include procedures that the AI data center or the cryptocurrency mining facility will utilize to, among other things, derive all electricity from new verifiable Class I renewable energy, energy from newly constructed nuclear power plants.

01/13/2026 - INTRODUCED.

NJ A 4872
2026
Rental Property Owners Competition Agreements
Status: Failed - Adjourned - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 1/8/2026
Author: Lopez (D) Additional Authors: Reynolds-Jackso (D);Sampson (D);Donlon (D);McCann Stamato (D);Peterpaul (D);Ramirez (D)
Topics: Housing
Associated Bills: (2024) NJ S 3699 - Identical
Summary: Prohibits certain coordination among residential rental property owners who restrict competition with respect to residential dwelling units, provides that it shall be unlawful and a violation of the New Jersey Antitrust Act for any agent or representative of a coordinator or subcontractor thereof to engage in consciously parallel pricing coordination.

2026

Status: Pending - Assembly Codes Committee

Author: Martinez (D)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Establishes that when an actor threatens to disseminate or publish a still or video image depicting such other person with one or more intimate parts exposed or engaging in sexual conduct with another person, including a still or video image created or altered by digitization, where such person may reasonably be identified from the still or video image itself or from information displayed in connection with the still or video image within the provisions of unlawful dissemination or publication.

Author: Woerner (D)

Summary: Allows certain interactive poker games be considered games of skill rather than games of luck, includes definitions, authorization, required safeguards and minimum standards, the scope of licensing review and state tax implications, makes corresponding penal law amendments.
History: Click for History
02/25/2025 - INTRODUCED.
02/25/2025 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on RACING AND WAGERING.
01/07/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on RACING AND WAGERING.

NY A 6545
2026
Liability for Damages Caused by Chatbot
Status: Pending - Assembly Science & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/7/2025
Author: Zaccaro (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Private Sector Use
Summary: Imposes liability for damages caused by a chatbot impersonating licensed professionals or giving any medical or psychological advice.
History: Click for History

NY A 6578
2026
Artificial Intelligence Training Data Transparency Act
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 2/24/2026
Author: Bores (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Establishes the artificial intelligence training data transparency act requiring developers of generative artificial intelligence models or services to post on the developer's website information regarding the data used by the developer to train the generative artificial intelligence model or service, includes a high-level summary of the datasets used in the development of such system or service.

NY A 6767
2026
Provision of an Artificial Intelligence Companion
Status: Pending - ASSEMBLY
Date of Last Action: 3/13/2025
Author: Vanel (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the provision of an artificial intelligence companion to a user unless such artificial intelligence companion contains a protocol for addressing possible suicidal ideation or self-harm expressed by a user, possible physical harm to others expressed by a user, and possible financial harm to others expressed by a user, requires certain notifications to certain users regarding crisis service providers and the non-human nature of such companion models.
History: Click for History

2026
Stop Deepfakes Act
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 6/6/2025
Author: Gounardes (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Government Use, Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Requires synthetic content creations system provider to apply provenance data, either directly or through the use of third-party technology, to synthetic content produced or modified by a synthetic content creations system that the synthetic content creations system provider makes available.

NY S 8589
2026
Automation Displacement Protection Act
Status: Pending - Senate Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: May (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Enacts the automation displacement protection act, relates to notice of technological displacement, provides that a covered employer shall provide no fewer than ninety days advance written notice prior to any technological displacement affecting twenty-five or more employees, or twenty-five percent of the workforce, whichever is less.

2026
Pricing in Food and Drug Retail Establishments
Status: Pending - Senate Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 12/12/2025
Author: Gianaris (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits the use of electronic shelving labels, digital shelf display technology, and personalized algorithmic pricing in food retail establishments and drug retail establishments, provides injunctive relief, civil penalties, and a private right of action.

Author: May (D)

Summary: Prohibits the use of algorithmically set prices, requires the disclosure of automated pricing systems, creates a private cause of action for violations of such prohibition.

NY S 8828
2026
Responsible AI Safety and Education (Raise) Act
Status: Enacted - Act No. 96
Date of Last Action: 03/27/2026 - Enacted
Author: Gounardes (D)
Topics: Cybersecurity, Notification, Oversight/Governance, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to transparency and safety requirements for developers of artificial intelligence frontier models, provides that a large frontier developer shall write, implement, comply with, and clearly and conspicuously publish on its internet website a frontier AI framework that applies to the large frontier developers frontier models and describes in detail how the large frontier developer handles, among other things, cybersecurity practices to secure unreleased model weights from unauthorized modification.

Author: Solages (D)

2026
Artificial Intelligence Workforce Impact Transparency
Status: Pending - Senate Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: Cooney (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Oversight/Governance, Private Sector Use
Summary: Enacts the Artificial Intelligence Workforce Impact Transparency, requires that each notice include a statement as to whether the employment losses described are the result, in whole or in part, of the introduction, expansion, or adoption of artificial intelligence systems, automation technologies, or machine-based processes that have replaced or materially altered the duties of affected employees.

2026
Employment Discrimination
Status: Pending - Senate Investigations and Government Operations Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Weik (R)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Prohibits employers from engaging in discrimination on the basis of a protected class when using artificial intelligence for recruitment, hiring, promotion, renewal of employment, selection for training or apprenticeship, discharge, discipline, tenure, or the terms, privileges, or conditions of employment.

2026
Artificial Intelligence Chatbots
Status: Pending - Senate Finance Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/27/2026
Author: Gonzalez (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits artificial intelligence chatbots from using features which are considered unsafe for minors, defines terms, specifies what are considered unsafe features, provides rights of action, directs rules and regulations to be established.

2026
Registration of Data Brokers
Status: Pending - Senate Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Gonzalez (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Requires the registration of data brokers, imposes regulations upon data brokers, establishes a data deletion mechanism for consumers, imposes penalties upon data brokers for violations of the law.
History: Click for History
01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To SENATE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.

NY S 9089
2026
Food Delivery Worker Safety and Fair Algorithms Act
Status: Pending - Senate Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Comrie (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Enacts the ""food delivery worker safety and fair algorithms act"" to prohibit food delivery platforms from utilizing or deploying any algorithmic management system that requires or incentivizes delivery workers to complete deliveries within a fixed or rigid time window that cannot reasonably be met while complying with all applicable traffic Laws and safety regulations, penalizes, deactivates, suspends, deprioritizes, or otherwise limits a delivery worker's access to work opportunities for failure to meet.
History: Click for History
01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/30/2026 - To SENATE Committee on LABOR.

NY A 9106
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence in Provision of Therapy
Status: Pending - Assembly Higher Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 9/26/2025
Author: Simon (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Regulates the use of artificial intelligence in the provision of therapy or psychotherapy services by prohibiting the use of artificial intelligence to assist in providing supplementary support where the session is recorded or transcribed unless the patient is informed of the specific purpose of such use and consents of such use, establishes penalties for violations of such provisions, excludes religious counseling, peer-support, and self-help materials and educational resources from such provisions.
History: Click for History
09/26/2025 - INTRODUCED.
09/26/2025 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION.
01/07/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on HIGHER EDUCATION.

2026
Data Broker Regulation
Status: Pending - Senate Internet and Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/5/2026
Author: Gonzalez (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Relates to data broker regulation, prohibits the acquisition of personally identifiable information through fraudulent means, requires a data broker to develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive information security program, requires data brokers operating in the state of New York to register with the attorney general.
History: Click for History

NY S 9390
2026
Civil, Criminal or Family Court Proceedings
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/6/2026
Author: Canzoneri-Fitz (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Judicial Use
Summary: Provides that in any civil, criminal or family court proceeding, where evidence is offered and a party contends that such evidence has been fabricated by means of generative artificial intelligence, the court shall not, on that ground alone, conduct an inquiry into such alleged fabrication unless the party so contending makes a showing sufficient to support a reasonable inference that the evidence may not be authentic.
History: Click for History
03/06/2026 - INTRODUCED.

2026
Department of Labor Reports
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2026
Author: Bronson (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Oversight/Governance, Private Sector Use, Studies
Summary: Requires covered businesses to annually report to the Department of Labor regarding the impact of artificial intelligence on hiring and business practices for the previous year, requires the Department of Labor to file an annual report on the impact of artificial intelligence on hiring and business practices in the State, establishes penalties for covered businesses that fail to submit such reports.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/21/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.
02/06/2026 - Amended in ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

NY S 9584
2026
Misleading Representations in Real Estate Listings
Status: Pending - Senate Consumer Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: May (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Housing, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Designates materially misleading digital representations in real estate listings as an unfair, deceptive or abusive act or false advertising, provides that a licensed real estate broker or salesperson shall be subject to suspension or revocation of their license for a violation of such provisions.
History: Click for History
03/26/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/26/2026 - To SENATE Committee on CONSUMER PROTECTION.

NY A 9601
2026
Use of Automated Systems to Make Employment Decisions
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Hooks (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of automated systems to make employment decisions unless there is a meaningful human review of the output of such automated system prior to the final employment decision, requires employers that use an automated system in the hiring process to notify applicants of the use of such system, provides that an applicant who has received an adverse employment decision may request a human-conducted review of such decision.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/21/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

NY A 9638
2026
Use of a Wage Fixing Algorithm
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Gallagher (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of a wage-fixing algorithm in combination with personal or behavioral data to set or recommend wages or compensation, defines terms, establishes penalties for violations of such prohibition.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/21/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

NY A 9641
2026
Algorithmic Wage Setting
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Torres (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits algorithmic wage-setting, provides for exceptions, provides for penalties for violation, creates a private right of action.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/21/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

NY A 9654
2026
State Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act
Status: Pending - Assembly Science & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/21/2026
Author: Solages (D)
Topics: Health Use, Impact Assessment, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action, Responsible Use, Studies
Summary: Enacts the State Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act establishing protections for individual rights with respect to computational algorithms, establishes protections against the use of algorithms involved in consequential decisions, such as those that impact people's rights, civil liberties, and livelihoods, including employment, banking, health care, the criminal justice system, public accommodations, and government services.
History: Click for History
01/21/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/21/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY.

NY S 9700
2026
Use of Algorithmic Device by Landlord to Determine Rent
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/2/2026
Author: Cleare (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Prohibits the use of an algorithmic device by a landlord for the purpose of determining the amount of rent to charge a residential tenant, declares that such use is an unfair or deceptive trade practice.
History: Click for History
04/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
04/02/2026 - To SENATE Committee on JUDICIARY.

Status: Pending - Assembly Ways and Means Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/9/2026
Author: Office of the Governor
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Provides for a recruitment incentive and retention program for certain active members of the New York Army National Guard, New York Air National Guard, and New York Naval Militia, amends the Tax Law, extends the suspension of the subsidy to the State Emergency Services Revolving Loan Fund from the public safety communications surcharge, relates to firearm prevention technology requirements for three dimensional printers, amends the Alcoholic Beverage Control Law, provides for various bonds and notes.

Author: Office of the Governor
Topics: Deepfake, Notification, Provenance
Summary: Increases certain motor vehicle transaction fees, establishes the Accident Prevention Course Internet Technology Pilot Program, allows for hire autonomous vehicles outside of New York City, relates to certain tax increment financing provisions, relates to dairy promotion and marketing of agricultural products, extends the refundability of the investment tax credit for farmers, relates to the Empire State Economic Development Fund, relates to ongoing treatment by an out of network provider during pregnancy.

NY A 10047
2026
Disclosure Requirements for AI Generated Media
Status: Pending - Assembly Consumer Affairs and Protection Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/30/2026
Author: Lavine (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Notification
Summary: Establishes disclosure requirements for AI-generated or materially AI-altered media depicting historically recognized human disasters when such media is publicly distributed, requires platform operators to allow the use of labelling, provides for enforcement by the attorney general.

01/30/2026 - INTRODUCED.

NY A 10118
2026
Food Delivery Worker Safety and Fair Algorithms Act
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Zinerman (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Enacts the ""food delivery worker safety and fair algorithms act"" to prohibit food delivery platforms from utilizing or deploying any algorithmic management system that requires or incentivizes delivery workers to complete deliveries within a fixed or rigid time window that cannot reasonably be met while complying with all applicable traffic Laws and safety regulations, penalizes, deactivates, suspends, deprioritizes, or otherwise limits a delivery worker's access to work opportunities for failure to meet.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/02/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

2026
Transitional Wage Insurance Program
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/12/2026
Author: Lasher (D)
Topics: Appropriations, Effect on Labor/Employment
Summary: Establishes the transitional wage insurance program under which the department of labor provides temporary supplemental payments to eligible individuals who accept new employment at a lower wage than their prior employment.
History: Click for History
02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/12/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on LABOR.

2026
Use of Automatic Data Systems with Employment
Status: Pending - Assembly Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/12/2026
Author: Solages (D)

Summary: Limits the use of automatic data systems in connection with employment, requires an employer shall provide a written notice that an automatic data system is being used, provides remedies.

02/12/2026 - INTRODUCED.

Summary: Requires certain disclosures by automobile insurers relating to the use of telematics systems in determining insurance rates and/or discounts.

NY A 10379
2026
Unsafe Chatbot Features for Minors
Status: Pending - Assembly Science & Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/23/2026
Author: Bores (D)
Topics: Health Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Prohibits artificial intelligence chatbots from using features which are considered unsafe for minors, provides that it shall be unlawful for a chatbot operator to provide unsafe chatbot features to a covered user unless the covered user is not a covered minor and the chatbot operator has used methods to determine that the covered user is not a covered minor, provides exceptions for certain advanced chatbots, relates to civil actions for violations.
History: Click for History
03/03/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/03/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY.
03/23/2026 - Amended in ASSEMBLY Committee on SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY.

NY A 10764
2026
Utility Billing Integrity Act
Status: Pending - Assembly Energy Committee
Date of Last Action: 4/1/2026
Author: Blumencranz (R)
Topics: Energy, Private Sector Use
Summary: Enacts the utility billing integrity act to establish utility billing integrity and consumer protections through anomaly detection, advanced data analytics and the usage of artificial intelligence, requires every utility to implement and maintain a billing integrity program utilizing anomaly detection systems to review all residential utility bills prior to issuance, provides that where a billing anomaly is identified, the utility shall provide prompt notice to the customer that the bill is under review.
History: Click for History
04/01/2026 - INTRODUCED.
04/01/2026 - To ASSEMBLY Committee on ENERGY.

2026

Author: Prather (D)

Date of Last Action: 3/26/2026
Author: Ranson (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Relates to children, enacts the Fostering the Future for State Children and Families Act, directs the Department of Human Services to carry out certain tasks within certain time period, provides that nothing in this act shall be construed to impair or affect authority or functions of certain departments or agencies, directs that act be implemented in accordance with applicable laws, directs the Department to submit annual report, provides for codification, provides an effective date.

OK H 3544
2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 3/25/2026
Author: Maynard (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Directs deployers, defined as persons, partnerships, corporations, or governmental entities that operate, control, or make available a social AI companion, to ensure social artificial intelligence companions are not knowingly made available to minors, permits lawful access by adults, directs deployers to implement protocols to prioritize the safety and well being of users, establishes civil penalties, grants the Attorney General rulemaking authority, provides for civil actions and penalties.

OK H 3545
2026
Artificial Intelligence
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/25/2026
Author: Maynard (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Government Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to technology, provides definitions, clarifies applicability of act, establishes prohibited uses of artificial intelligence (AI) by state agencies, establishes allowed uses of AI and applicable restrictions, directs state agencies to take action and report compliance to the Office of Management and Enterprise Services (OMES), requires future AI systems comply with act, directs OMES to provide report on AI systems used by state agencies, provides for codification, provides an effective date.

OK H 3546
2026
Artificial Intelligence Systems
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/24/2026
Author: Maynard (R)
Topics: Personhood
Summary: Relates to technology, prohibits the granting of personhood to artificial intelligence systems, environmental elements, nonhuman animals, and inanimate objects, clarifies act does not revoke personhood of entities currently recognized, provides for codification, provides an effective date.

2026
Parent Data Sovereignty Act
Status: Pending - House Rules Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Maynard (R)
Topics: Education Use, Provenance
Summary: Relates to schools, creates the Parent Data Sovereignty Act of 2026, declares legislative findings and intent, defines terms, clarifies ownership of certain student data, prohibits student data use for commercial purposes, declares parental rights to obtain student data and opt out of certain data collection activities, prohibits collection of student data under certain circumstances without parental consent, requires legislative approval for certain data collection.

2026

Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Provenzano (D)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Relates to health insurance, provides definitions, prohibits utilization review agents from using an automated system to make adverse determinations, permits Insurance Commissioner auditing authority, clarifies act does not prohibit use of automated decision systems for administrative support or fraud detection, directs notice of adverse determinations to include certain information, provides for codification, provides an effective date.

OK H 3959
2026
Protecting Consumers and Jobs from Predatory Pricing
Status: Pending - House Business and Commerce Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Munson (D)
Topics: Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to technology, creates the Protecting Consumers and Jobs from Predatory Pricing Act, provides definitions, directs food retail establishments that use personalized algorithmic pricing using specific consumer data to make disclosure to consumers, prohibits the use of electronic shelving labels by large food retailers, clarifies what is not limited, prohibits the use of electronic shelving labels for personalized algorithmic pricing or surveillance pricing.

Summary: Relates to technology, provides definitions, directs deployers of chatbots to ensure AI chatbots do not make human-like features available to minors, directs deployers to implement reasonable age verification systems, permits deployers to provide alternative versions of chatbot without human-like features, directs deployers to ensure social AI companions are not available to minors, provides exemptions for certain therapeutic chatbots.

Author: Moore (R)
Topics: Judicial Use
Summary: Relates to artificial intelligence in civil proceedings, relates to the duty of an attorney and legal advisor, provides that an attorney shall never knowingly encourage or produce false, misleading, or manipulated evidence including evidence artificially generated or altered to be false, misleading, or manipulated, provides that an attorney shall never knowingly cite to fictitious court authorities.

OR S 1546

Date of Last Action: 03/31/2026 - Enacted
Author: Early Childhood and Behavioral Health Cmt
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Summary: Requires operators of artificial intelligence companions and artificial intelligence companion platforms to provide notice to users that the users are interacting with artificial output if a reasonable person that interacts with the artificial intelligence companion or artificial intelligence companion platform would believe that the person was interacting with a natural person.

RI S 2129
2026
Healthcare Data Privacy
Status: Pending - Senate Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/16/2026
Author: LaMountain (D)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use, Provenance
Summary: Classifies research, intent and interest in healthcare services as protected healthcare data, sets forth additional responsibilities for regulated entities that seek to collect and share consumer data including a requirement for specific and conspicuous consumer consent, requires separate conspicuous consent from the consumer to sell this data, stipulates acceptable uses for the data and what rights the consumer has regarding their data.
History: Click for History
01/16/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/16/2026 - To SENATE Committee on JUDICIARY.

2026
Artificial Intelligence Companion Models
Status: Pending - Senate Artificial Intelligence & Emerging Technologies Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Urso (D)
Topics: Health Use, Impact Assessment
Summary: Creates additional safety features for artificial intelligence (AI) companion technology including provisions addressing suicidal ideation, potential physical harm or financial harm to others expressed by a user, requires notification that the AI companion does not have human emotions.

2026

Date of Last Action: 1/15/2026
Author: Lombardi (D)

Summary: Requires the department of administration to provide an inventory of all state agencies using artificial intelligence and establishes a thirteen member permanent commission to monitor the use of artificial intelligence in state government and make recommendations for state government policy and other decisions, directs the commission to make recommendations regarding changes in the way state government uses artificial intelligence.

2026

Status: Pending - House Innovation, Internet and Technology Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2026
Author: Voas (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Deepfake, Private Sector Use
Summary: Requires that video or photography generated by artificial intelligence or AI that is posted on a public platform and that is viewable in Rhode Island shall contain a marking disclosing that the video or photo has been generated by AI, provides that a violation of this provision by an entity for profit would constitute a deceptive trade practice.

SC S 734
2026
Criminal Sexual Misconduct
Status: Pending - Senate Finance Committee

Author: Kennedy (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake

SC S 788
2026
Artificial Intelligence and Therapy or Psychotherapy
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Blackmon (R) Additional Authors: Stubbs (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification
Summary: Provides definitions related to artificial intelligence and therapy or psychotherapy, provides that a licensed professional shall not be permitted to use artificial intelligence to assist in providing supplementary support in therapy or psychotherapy where the client's therapeutic session is recorded unless the patient is informed that artificial intelligence will be used and for what purpose and the patient consents to this use.

2026

Status: Pending - Senate Banking and Insurance Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/26/2026
Author: Leber (R)
Topics: Impact Assessment, Notification, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Enacts the consumer protections in interactions with artificial intelligence systems act, among other things, prohibit algorithmic discrimination arising from the use of high-risk artificial intelligence systems.

02/26/2026 - INTRODUCED.

SC S 1037
2026
Protecting Children from Chatbots Act
Status: Pending - Senate Labor, Commerce and Industry Committee
Date of Last Action: 3/19/2026
Author: Kimbrell (R) Additional Authors: Adams (R);Garrett (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Criminal Use, Deepfake, Health Use
Summary: Enacts the Protecting Children from Chatbots Act, defines terms related to chat bot usage, provides that a covered entity shall make a limited-access mode of a chatbot available and verify the user's age, provides that if a parent provides consent, then a minor shall be able to use a chatbot in limited-access mode or access restricted features, prohibits a covered entity from prioritizing engagement at the expense of the user's well-being.
History: Click for History
03/19/2026 - INTRODUCED.
03/19/2026 - To SENATE Committee on LABOR, COMMERCE AND INDUSTRY.

SC H 3431
2026
Covered Online Services Requirements
Status: Enacted - Act No. 96
Date of Last Action: 02/05/2026 - Enacted
Author: Newton (R) Additional Authors: Erickson (R);Crawford H (R);Bernstein (D);Bradley (R);Calhoon (R);Oremus (R);Moore T (R);Smith M (R);Robbins (R);Guffey (R);Govan (D);Hartz (R)
Topics: Private Sector Use
Summary: Provides that a covered online service shall exercise reasonable care in the use of minors' personal data, provides for certain requirements for covered online services, restricts the amount of personal data of a minor that may be collected, provides for parental controls, provides for an annual report, provides for enforcement.

Tennessee
TN S 677
2026
Department of Education
Status: Pending - Senate Select Committee on Calendar
Date of Last Action: 1/31/2025
Author: White (R)
Topics: Education Use, Education/Training
Associated Bills: TN H 545 - Same as
Summary: Requires the department to provide at least one professional development course that provides asynchronous instruction on the use of artificial intelligence in the classroom and that is made available, at no cost, to teachers in grades 6-12, requires such teachers to complete at least one department-approved professional development course on the use of artificial intelligence in the classroom by the specified date, or within two years of becoming licensed as a teacher in this state, whichever is later.
History: Click for History

TN S 1363
2026
Consumer Protection
Status: Pending - Senate Commerce and Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/6/2025
Author: Watson B (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Oversight/Governance
Associated Bills: TN H 1246 - Same as
Summary: Requires the office of the attorney general and reporter, submits to the Chair of the House Committee having jurisdiction over consumer protection and the Chair of the Commerce and Labor Committee of the senate a report on the number of investigations into violations of the State Consumer Protection Act of 1977 in which artificial intelligence was used to effectuate an unfair or deceptive act or practice affecting the conduct of any trade or commerce between the specified date, and a specified date.
History: Click for History

TN H 1455
2026
Criminal Offenses
Status: Pending - House Judiciary Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Littleton (R)
Topics: Criminal Use, Health Use, Private Sector Use, Private Right of Action
Associated Bills: TN S 1493 - Same as
Summary: Creates a Class A felony offense of knowingly training artificial intelligence to encourage the act of suicide or criminal homicide, or act in specific manners, including developing an emotional relationship with an individual or simulating a human being, including in appearance, voice, or other mannerisms.

02/05/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on COMMERCE: Referred to Subcommittee on BANKING & CONSUMER AFFAIRS.

TN H 2416
2026
Use of Artificial Intelligence by Students
Status: Pending - House Education Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Stevens (R)
Topics: Education Use
Associated Bills: TN S 2105 - Same as
Summary: Requires each public charter school to publish the policy adopted by its governing board on the use of artificial intelligence by students, teachers, and staff for instructional and assignment purposes on its website, requires each local board of education to direct each school under its management and control to publish the policy adopted by the local board on the use of artificial intelligence by students, teachers, and staff for instructional and assignment purposes on the school's website.
History: Click for History
02/03/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on EDUCATION.
02/05/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on EDUCATION: Subcommittee on EDUCATION INSTRUCTION.

TN S 2521
2026
State Government
Status: Pending - Senate Commerce and Labor Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Yarbro (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Studies
Associated Bills: TN H 2586 - Same as
Summary: Expands the requirements of the 2026 and 2027 annual reports to be submitted by the artificial intelligence advisory council to the governor, speaker of the senate, and speaker of the house of representatives by requiring such reports to include recommendations on strategies to detect and minimize the risk of fraud committed by persons using artificial intelligence.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on COMMERCE AND LABOR.
03/10/2026 - In SENATE Committee on COMMERCE AND LABOR: Referred to General Subcommittee.

2026
State Government
Status: Pending - Held on Desk
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Chism (D)
Topics: Criminal Use, Studies
Associated Bills: TN S 2521 - Same as
Summary: Expands the requirements of the 2026 and 2027 annual reports to be submitted by the Artificial Intelligence Advisory Council to the Governor, speaker of the Senate, and speaker of the House of Representatives by requiring such reports to include recommendations on strategies to detect and minimize the risk of fraud committed by persons using artificial intelligence.
History: Click for History
02/03/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - In HOUSE. Read second time. Caption bill held on desk.

TN S 2618
2026
Computers and Electronic Processing
Status: Pending - SENATE
Date of Last Action: 2/2/2026
Author: Yarbro (D)
Topics: Impact Assessment, Effect on Labor/Employment, Responsible Use, Studies
Summary: Requires the joint chairs of the artificial intelligence advisory council to publish their annual findings regarding the economic, labor, and ethical impacts of artificial intelligence on an appropriate page of the general assembly's website, in addition to the existing requirement to submit these reports to the governor and legislative speakers.
History: Click for History
02/02/2026 - INTRODUCED.
02/05/2026 - To SENATE Committee on DELAYED BILLS.

TN H 2619
2026
Insurance Agents and Policies
Status: Pending - House Finance, Ways & Means Committee
Date of Last Action: 2/3/2026
Author: Howell (R)
Topics: Health Use, Private Sector Use
Associated Bills: TN S 2155 - Same as
Summary: Establishes the State commission of insurance review, prohibits a health insurance entity from offering or maintaining a health benefit plan that uses downcoding in a manner that prevents the provider from collecting the fee for actual services performed either from the health benefit plan or the patient, makes other changes related to health insurance and health insurers.

UT S 256
2026
Identity Protection Modifications
Status: Enacted - Act No. 489
Date of Last Action: 03/26/2026 - Enacted
Author: Cullimore (R) Additional Authors: Teuscher (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Notification
Summary: Amends provisions related to libel and slander to address artificial intelligence and digitally manipulated content, clarifies that defamation law applies to content created through artificial intelligence or other technological means, requires notice to a publisher before filing a defamation action based on digitally created content, limits recovery to actual damages if the publisher removes the content within 10 days after receiving notice.

2026
Software in Education Amendments
Status: Enacted - Act No. 161
Date of Last Action: 03/18/2026 - Enacted
Author: Cullimore (R) Additional Authors: Fiefia (R)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Requires the State Board of Education to conduct a study regarding the use of software and digital services in public schools, review best practices related to the educational effectiveness, design, and data practices of software that students use, publish guidance describing best practices for the responsible use of software in a public school, and upon request, report the findings of the study to the Education Interim Committee, relates to best practices related to student learning, safety, and privacy.

2026
Classroom Technology Amendments
Status: Enacted - Act No. 120
Date of Last Action: 03/18/2026 - Enacted
Author: Defay (R) Additional Authors: Wilson (R)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Requires the State Board of Education to create model policies on the use of technology and artificial intelligence in a public school classroom, defines terms, requires the State Board of Education to include certain technology standards in core education standards, creates certain requirements for a local education agency related to the use of technology in the classroom, requires the state board to create a model policy on the balanced use of technology in the classroom and use of AI in the classroom.

UT H 276
2026
Artificial Intelligence Modifications
Status: Enacted - Act No. 352
Date of Last Action: 03/24/2026 - Enacted
Author: Defay (R) Additional Authors: Cullimore (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Notification, Private Right of Action, Provenance
Summary: Enacts provisions related to counterfeit intimate images and digital content provenance standards, defines terms, creates the Digital Voyeurism Prevention Act to address non-consensual generation and distribution of counterfeit intimate images, prohibits generation services and platforms from distributing counterfeit intimate images without obtaining and verifying consent from the depicted individual, establishes civil liability for violations of the act.

UT H 289
2026
Child Sexual Abuse Material Amendments
Status: Enacted - Act No. 358
Date of Last Action: 03/24/2026 - Enacted
Author: Defay (R) Additional Authors: Musselman (R)
Topics: Child Pornography, Deepfake
Summary: Addresses offenses related to child sexual abuse material, defines the term of apparent child sexual abuse material to address material created or generated by artificial intelligence that depicts realistic minors, defines the term of obscene child sexual abuse material, amends the definition of child sexual abuse material to remove artificially generated material.

2026

Status: Enacted - Act No. 240
Date of Last Action: 03/19/2026 - Enacted
Author: Johnson (R) Additional Authors: Hall K (R)
Topics: Health Use, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Amends requirements for health insurance preauthorization, requires an insurer to post certain information regarding pre-authorizations and preauthorization requirements on the insurer's website, requires an insurer to disclose whether the insurer uses artificial intelligence in the process of reviewing a request for authorization, defines a maximum time in which an insurer is required to make an authorization or adverse preauthorization determination.

VA HJR 32
2026
Artificial Intelligence Use Policies
Status: Adopted - Adopted
Date of Last Action: 03/03/2026 - Enacted
Author: Laufer (D) Additional Authors: Price (D);Tran (D);Maldonado (D);Shin (D);Cousins (D)
Topics: Education Use, Studies
Summary: Relates to a study, relates to the Joint Legislative Audit and Review Commission, relates to artificial intelligence use policies at institutions of higher education in the state, relates to a report, directs the Joint Legislative Audit and Review Commission to study artificial intelligence use policies at institutions of higher education in the state and evaluate each policy regarding academic integrity, data privacy, and equity and access.

VA S 141
2026
Political Campaign Advertisements
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 3/12/2026
Author: Salim (D) Additional Authors: Surovell (D);Srinivasan (D);Perry (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Elections
Summary: Relates to elections, relates to political campaign advertisements, relates to synthetic media, relates to penalty, prohibits electioneering communications containing synthetic media, as those terms are defined in the bill, from being published or broadcast without containing the following conspicuously displayed statement: 'This message contains synthetic media that has been altered from its original source or artificially generated and may present conduct or speech that did not occur.

VA H 171
2026
School Internet Safety
Status: Enacted - Act No. 48
Date of Last Action: 04/02/2026 - Enacted
Author: Askew (D) Additional Authors: Price (D);Keys-Gamarra (D)
Topics: Deepfake, Education Use, Education/Training
Summary: Provides that every two years, each local school board shall review, amend if necessary, and approve the school division's acceptable use policy for the internet, contains provisions that are internet safety for students that is integrated in a division's instructional program that includes instruction on key modern digital safety topics, including online scams, misinformation, and content generated by artificial intelligence.

Date of Last Action: 1/13/2026
Author: Pekarsky (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to fostering Access, Innovation, and Responsibility in Artificial Intelligence Act established, establishes the Fostering Access, Innovation, and Responsibility in Artificial Intelligence Act (FAIR AI Act) that requires a developer of a base artificial intelligence model, as defined in the bill, to clearly and conspicuously disclose, in a manner that is appropriate for the medium of the content and is easily accessible to the user of such model.

VA S 429
2026
Nursing Home Oversight and Accountability
Status: To Governor - Eligible for Governor
Date of Last Action: 2/27/2026
Author: Head (R) Additional Authors: Mulchi (R)
Topics: Health Use
Summary: Provides that if any required visits to a resident by a physician do not take place in accordance with the federal physician visit schedule, any nursing home shall send notice to the resident, the residents family or legal representative, and the Department of Health, provides that all licensed nursing homes shall conduct comprehensive assessments of each residents needs, provides that a nursing home shall develop a comprehensive plan of care for each resident.

Summary: Relates to artificial Intelligence Chatbots Act established, relates to prohibited practices, relates to penalties, creates the Artificial Intelligence Chatbots Act, which prohibits an operator from making a companion chatbot, as those terms are defined in the bill, available to a user in the Commonwealth unless the companion chatbot is incapable of certain actions specified in the bill.

Status: Failed - Died

Author: Maldonado (D) Additional Authors: Shin (D);Glass (D);Anthony (D)

Summary: Relates to professions and occupations, relates to impersonation of certain licensed professionals by chatbot, relates to notice, relates to civil liability, provides that a proprietor that owns, operates, or deploys a chatbot, defined in the bill, shall not permit such chatbot to provide any substantive response, information, or advice, or take any action that, if taken by a natural person, would constitute the unlawful practice of architecture, engineering, surveying, landscape architecture, and geology.

VA H 758
2026
Artificial Intelligence Chatbots and Minors Act
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Runion (R) Additional Authors: McLaughlin (R);Oates (R)
Topics: Deepfake, Health Use
Summary: Relates to Artificial Intelligence Chatbots and Minors Act established, relates to prohibited practices, relates to penalties, creates the Artificial Intelligence Chatbots and Minors Act to require that deployers that operate or distribute a chatbot in the Commonwealth ensure that any chatbot operated or distributed by the deployer does not make human-like features, defined in the bill, available to minors to use, interact with, purchase, or converse with and implement reasonable age verification.

VA H 999
2026
State Human Rights Act
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Cole (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to Virginia Human Rights Act, relates to equal credit opportunities, relates to Virginia Fair Housing Law, relates to nondiscrimination by automated decision systems, provides that it is an unlawful discriminatory practice for any person to deploy, use, or rely on an automated decision system to make a decision pursuant to the Virginia Human Rights Act, provisions related to equal credit opportunities.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on GENERAL LAWS.

02/18/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on APPROPRIATIONS: Left in committee.

VA H 1170
2026
Covered Artificial Intelligence Systems
Status: Failed - Tabled in Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Carroll (D) Additional Authors: Maldonado (D)
Topics: Government Use, Studies
Summary: Relates to Department of Criminal Justice Services, relates to law-enforcement agencies and sheriff's departments, relates to policy on use of covered artificial intelligence systems, requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish a model policy for the use of a covered artificial intelligence system, defined in the bill, by any state or local law-enforcement agency or sheriff's department by specified date.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.

2026
School Board Policies
Status: To Governor - Eligible for Governor
Date of Last Action: 3/13/2026
Author: Rasoul (D) Additional Authors: Maldonado (D)
Topics: Education Use
Summary: Relates to Department of Education, relates to artificial intelligence use in instructional settings, relates to development of AIS safety guidance required, relates to AIS Innovation in Education Pilot Program established, relates to report, compiles information on current uses of artificial intelligence systems for student instruction in public schools in the Commonwealth, establishes and posts in a publicly accessible location on its website guidance for the safe, ethical, and equitable use of an AIS.

VA H 1250
2026
Technology Governance and Coordination Program
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Anthony (D)
Topics: Impact Assessment, Studies
Summary: Relates to Department of Law, relates to Technology Governance and Coordination Program, relates to report, directs the Office of the Attorney General to establish a Technology Governance and Coordination Program to support the Commonwealth's response to emergent technologies, including artificial intelligence, algorithmic systems, biometric systems, and automated decision-making tools, requires the Office of the Attorney General to submit an annual report to the Joint Commission on Technology.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.

VA H 1252
2026
State Residential Landlord and Tenant Act
Status: Pending - Carryover - House General Laws Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Anthony (D)
Topics: Housing, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relates to Virginia Residential Landlord and Tenant Act, relates to algorithmic pricing device use by certain landlords, relates to civil penalties, requires a landlord, defined in the bill as a landlord who owns more than 10 rental dwelling units or more than a 10 percent interest in more than 10 rental dwelling units, whether individually or through a business entity, in the Commonwealth, who uses an algorithmic pricing device, defined in the bill, to establish the advertised rent.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on GENERAL LAWS.
02/03/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on GENERAL LAWS: Continued to 2027.

VA H 1257
2026
Law Enforcement Agencies
Status: Pending - Carryover - House Communications, Technology and Innovation Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Glass (D)
Topics: Government Use, Studies
Summary: Relates to law-enforcement agencies, relates to use of certain technologies and interrogation practices, relates to forensic laboratory accreditation, directs the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish a comprehensive framework for the use of generative artificial intelligence, machine learning systems, audiovisual surveillance technologies, and custodial and noncustodial interrogations of adults and juveniles by law-enforcement agencies.
History: Click for History
01/14/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/14/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION.
02/02/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION: Continued to 2027.

2026

Status: Pending - Carryover - House Communications, Technology and Innovation Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/14/2026
Author: Glass (D)
Topics: Government Use, Studies
Summary: Relates to law-enforcement agencies, relates to use of certain technologies and interrogation practices, relates to forensic laboratory accreditation, directs the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish a comprehensive framework for the use of generative artificial intelligence systems, machine learning systems, audiovisual surveillance technologies, and custodial and noncustodial interrogations of adults and juveniles by law-enforcement agencies.

VA H 1294
2026
Artificial Intelligence Based Tools
Status: Pending - Carryover - House Communications, Technology and Innovation Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/15/2026
Author: Clark D (D) Additional Authors: Maldonado (D)
Topics: Government Use, Notification
Summary: Relates to use of artificial intelligence-based tools, relates to covered artificial intelligence, relates to disclosure of use, requires the use of covered artificial intelligence, as defined in the bill, in a criminal investigation to be disclosed in a police report filed for that investigation, provides that such a report shall be submitted to the attorney for the Commonwealth upon arrest or issuance of a summons and made available to the individual under investigation or such individual's counsel.
History: Click for History
01/15/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/15/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION.
02/02/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION: Continued to 2027.

VA H 1295
2026
Law Enforcement
Status: Pending - Carryover - House Communications, Technology and Innovation Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/15/2026
Author: Clark D (D) Additional Authors: Maldonado (D)
Topics: Government Use
Summary: Relates to law enforcement, requires all law-enforcement agencies, defined in the bill as any state or local law-enforcement agency or sheriff's department, to conduct an inventory of any covered artificial intelligence system, defined in the bill, used by such agency and to make such inventory publicly available by a specified date of each year, provides that the Attorney General may investigate and, if warranted, bring a civil action against any law-enforcement agency.
History: Click for History
01/15/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/15/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION.
02/02/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on COMMUNICATIONS, TECHNOLOGY AND INNOVATION: Continued to 2027.

VA H 1451
2026
Labor and Employment
Status: Pending - Carryover - House Labor and Commerce Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/22/2026
Author: Anthony (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification
Summary: Relates to labor and employment, relates to warehouse employers, relates to required disclosures and recordkeeping, relates to civil penalties, requires each employer of 500 or more warehouse employees in the Commonwealth to provide a written description of each quota to which an employee is subject, any incentive or bonus associated with meeting the quota, and any potential adverse employment action that may result from failure to meet the quota.
History: Click for History
01/22/2026 - INTRODUCED.
01/22/2026 - To HOUSE Committee on LABOR AND COMMERCE.
02/10/2026 - In HOUSE Committee on LABOR AND COMMERCE: Continued to 2027.

VA H 1514
2026
Employment Decisions
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 1/23/2026
Author: Tran (D)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment, Notification, Private Sector Use
Summary: Relates to employment decisions, relates to automated decision systems, relates to civil penalty, provides that the Director of the Department of Human Resource Management shall require any state agency that uses an automated decision system as a substantial factor in any employment decision, as those terms are defined in the bill, to ensure that such system and the use of such system complies with federal and state law, make certain disclosures, provide an opt-out mechanism, annually test such system.
History: Click for History

Author: Williams (R)

Summary: Relates to digital innovation and infrastructure, relates to establishing rights in digital property and technology resources, relates to requiring risk management policies for critical infrastructure facilities controlled by critical artificial intelligence systems, relates to providing safe harbors, relates to preempting local regulation, relates to and providing for enforcement and remedies.

WA H 1170
2026
Developed or Modified by Artificial Intelligence
Status: Enacted - Act No. 167
Date of Last Action: 03/24/2026 - Enacted
Author: Shavers (D) Additional Authors: Ormsby (D);Ryu (D);Wylie (D);Pollet (D);Ramel (D);Taylor (D);Fosse (D);Hill (D)
Topics: Notification, Private Sector Use, Responsible Use
Summary: Relates to informing users when content is developed or modified by artificial intelligence, provides that a government agency that makes available an artificial intelligence system intended to interact with consumers must disclose to each consumer, before or at the time of interaction, that the consumer is interacting with an artificial intelligence system.

2026

Status: Enacted - Act No. 157
Date of Last Action: 03/23/2026 - Enacted
Author: Orwall (D) Additional Authors: Hasegawa (D);Lovelett (D);Muzzall (R);Nobles (D)
Topics: Health Use
Associated Bills: WA H 1566 - Companion
Summary: Makes improvements to transparency and accountability in the prior authorization determination process, provides that when denying a prior authorization determination, the carrier shall include the credentials, board certifications, and areas of specialty of the provider who had clinical oversight over the determination in any notification sent to the health plan enrollee and provider requesting or referring the service.

WA S 5886
2026
Forged Digital Likenesses
Status: Enacted - Act No. 69
Date of Last Action: 03/16/2026 - Enacted
Author: Boehnke (R)
Topics: Deepfake
Summary: Provides that every individual or personality has a property right in the use of his or her name, voice, signature, photograph, forged digital likeness, or likeness, provides penalty and liable for actual damages, provides that if the infringement is of a living or deceased individual's or personality's forged digital likeness, the infringing party shall also be liable for the noneconomic damages sustained as a result of the infringement whether or not the infringement.

WV H 4014
2026

Status: Failed - Adjourned - House Finance Committee
Date of Last Action: 1/28/2026
Author: Hanshaw (R)
Topics: Effect on Labor/Employment
Associated Bills: WV S 402 - Similar to
Summary: Relates to expanding opportunities and participation in the states workforce through the Workforce Readiness and Opportunity Act, creates the West Virginia Micro-credential Program under the authority of the West Virginia Higher Education Policy Commission, expands the availability for the tax credit for apprenticeship training programs, provides additional modification from personal and corporate net income tax for contributions and recipients to voluntary portable benefits plans.

Wyoming
WY H 91
2026
Ban on Government Social Scoring With AI
Status: Failed - Died
Date of Last Action: 2/10/2026
Author: Yin (D) Additional Authors: Rothfuss (D);Provenza (D)
Topics: Government Use, Notification
Summary: Relates to administration of the government, prohibits government entities from using artificial intelligence to calculate and assign social scores, prohibits government entities from using artificial intelligence to identify persons using biometric data or gathering of images, provides exceptions, provides definitions, provides for an effective date.
